ways (WinUI OffsetFromCenter); otherwise it reveals from the anchor edge.</li><li><xref href="FluentGpu.Controls.PopupChrome.Raw" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref> — ToolTip/Slider value tip: FadeIn/FadeOutThemeAnimation (TAS_FADEIN/TAS_FADEOUT, 167ms linear).</li><li><xref href="FluentGpu.Controls.PopupChrome.Modal" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref> — ContentDialog scale/fade; <xref href="FluentGpu.Controls.PopupChrome.TeachingTip" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref> — muxc expand/contract scale.</li><li><xref href="FluentGpu.Controls.PopupChrome.Static" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref> — a bare WinUI <code>Popup</code> with no transitions (the AutoSuggestBox SuggestionsPopup,
generic.xaml AutoSuggestBox template — no TransitionCollection and AutoSuggestBox_Partial.cpp adds none): instant.</li></ul>

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.PopupOptions
files.Summary: Optional popup behavior. keeps Tab/Shift-Tab inside the overlay subtree (modal-style).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.PopupPlacementResult
files.Summary: Result of placing an anchored popup: the absolute top-left, which side it opened on (so the open animation can reveal from the correct edge), the width/height that actually fit (after a full collision resize), the EFFECTIVE placement after fallback (WinUI PerformPlacementWithFallback reports the placement that won), and the corner-join against the anchor.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.ProgressBar.ProgressBarTemplateSettings
files.Summary: The computed translate positions WinUI binds from ProgressBarTemplateSettings into the indeterminate storyboards (ProgressBar.cpp UpdateWidthBasedTemplateSettings). Indicator widths follow SetProgressBarIndicatorWidth.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.ProgressBar
files.Summary: A WinUI ProgressBar (1:1). A 3px-min band (ProgressBarMinHeight) holding a 1px track (ProgressBarTrackHeight, ControlStrongStrokeColorDefault) under an accent indicator (AccentFillColorDefaultBrush) with a 1.5px corner. fills the indicator to a 0..1 value; sweeps the two clipped accent indicators across the track on the looping translate keyframes WinUI binds from ProgressBarTemplateSettings (Container/Container2 positions). Paused/Error stop the sweep and settle a full-width bar at ContainerAnimationMidPosition, recolored to SystemFillColorCaution / SystemFillColorCritical (instant recolor — no fill-color anim channel).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.ProgressBarState
files.Summary: The drawing state of a — mirrors WinUI's CommonStates (Determinate / Paused / Error and their Indeterminate counterparts), selected from IsIndeterminate + ShowPaused + ShowError.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.ProgressRing.ProgressRingTemplateSettings
files.Summary: Computed geometry for one ProgressRing (the audit's P3 TemplateSettings convention — mirrors WinUI's ProgressRingTemplateSettings, recomputed once from the control's size, never inside a per-frame bind). Sweep is the determinate accent-arc sweep in degrees (value·360). For the indeterminate ring it is the fixed visible arc length the rotation loop spins.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.ProgressRing
files.Summary:
A WinUI ProgressRing brought to 1:1 with controls/dev/ProgressRing. WinUI renders the ring via a Lottie AnimatedVisualPlayer (a continuous accent ring, NOT dots): a determinate variant whose accent arc's TrimEnd sweeps 0→1 over a (default-transparent) track ring, and an indeterminate variant whose accent arc rotates while its trim breathes. We reproduce both on the engine's SDF arc primitive () — the determinate via a -computed sweep, the indeterminate via a rotation Keyframes loop at the exact WinUI cadence (900° per 2.0s, two cubic-bezier 0.167,0.167→0.833,0.833 half-segments).

Dimensional canon (ProgressRing.xaml / AnimatedVisuals/ProgressRingIndeterminate.cpp): Width=Height=32 default, MinWidth=MinHeight=16. Stroke is the Lottie weight, NOT the unused ProgressRingStrokeThickness=4 themeresource: the indeterminate ellipse uses StrokeThickness(1.5F) inside an 80px viewbox at Scale:5,5 (effective 1.5·5 = 7.5 in the viewbox), which maps to the 32px control as 7.5·32/80 = 3.0px (= size·0.09375). We scale it proportionally so larger rings keep the same visual weight. Foreground=AccentFillColorDefaultBrush (Tok.AccentDefault), Background=ControlFillColorTransparentBrush (transparent — the default track is invisible, exactly like WinUI). IsActive=false → the LayoutRoot fades to Opacity 0 (the Inactive visual state).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.RadioButton
files.Summary: A WinUI RadioButton — style values from microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\CommonStyles\RadioButton_themeresources.xaml ("the template"; ThemeDictionaries Default=dark :4-61 / Light :120-177): a 20px ring (OuterEllipse/CheckOuterEllipse, :371/373) + (when selected) a TextOnAccent dot (CheckGlyph, :374) that grows 12→14 on hover / shrinks →10 on press over ControlNormalAnimationDuration (250ms) with the ControlFastOutSlowInKeySpline (:255-260/:292-297), plus the unchecked-pressed 4px proto-dot (PressedCheckGlyph, :376) revealing toward 10 over ControlFastAnimationDuration (167ms, :298-306). Mutual exclusion comes from a shared selected index — use (an ad-hoc set) or the full container (header, columns, roving keyboard). Controlled.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.RadioButtons
files.Summary:
The WinUI RadioButtons container (microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\RadioButtons): a header + a column-major grid of mutually-exclusive items with the container keyboard contract.

Layout = ColumnMajorUniformToLargestGridLayout (RadioButtons.xaml:22-26): items flow top-to-bottom column-major into at most maxColumns columns — the first count % columns columns take one extra item (ColumnMajorUniformToLargestGridLayout.cpp ArrangeOverride :48-120; CalculateColumns clamps to min(MaxColumns, count), :132-163 — the available-width clamp needs measure and is not applied here). ColumnSpacing = 7, RowSpacing = 8, header margin 0,0,0,8 (RadioButtons_themeresources.xaml:18-20); header foreground TextFillColorPrimary / Disabled (:4-10).

Keyboard (RadioButtons.cpp): ONE tab stop — the container is IsTabStop=False + TabNavigation=Once (RadioButtons.xaml:5-6) and focus entering the group lands on the selected item (OnGettingFocus redirect, :80-97); here only the selected (or first) item is Focusable, the roving-tab-stop equivalent. Up/Down move focus ±1 in data order (OnChildPreviewKeyDown :135-165 → MoveFocus, :414-447); Left/Right move across columns (:167-183, the XYFocus directional move constrained to the repeater); SELECTION FOLLOWS FOCUS unless Ctrl is held (:100-107). At the edges the key is swallowed when the source is the first/last item (HandleEdgeCaseFocus, :216-242 — WinUI then tries to move focus OUT of the group, which a control factory cannot do; deviation noted). Controlled.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.RatingControl
files.Summary:
A WinUI RatingControl: a row of stars set by click or press-and-sweep, with an optional caption. The control is a two-layer star row -- an always-present unset/background layer (outline glyph E734) and a clipped Set layer (filled glyph E735) revealed to the current rating. Faithful port of RatingControl.cpp:

  • State model -- (caller ; -1 == unset), an unset shown until a real value is set, and an optional .
  • Pointer preview -- press-and-sweep fills the stars to the pointer (with a partial tail) and lifts the foreground to the pointer-over visual-state color; release commits. clears the rating when you click the star equal to the current value. Implicit capture keeps the sweep alive OFF the strip while pressed, so dragging past the LEFT edge and releasing clears to (RatingControl.cpp:799-805, 856-863, 888-906). A TOUCH press lifts the hover-scale scalar to c_touchOverScale = 1.0 (vs mouse 0.8) so the focal star grows to full size under the finger.
  • Keyboard -- Left/Down -1, Right/Up +1, Home clears, End sets (RatingControl::OnKeyDown).
  • Read-only / disabled -- renders a fixed rating with no interaction; =false dims the foreground to the disabled brush and gates all input.

Custom font/image item-info (RatingItemFontInfo/RatingItemImageInfo per-state glyphs/images) is deferred.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.RatingControlTemplateSettings
files.Summary:
Computed template settings for the (the typed-record convention -- see / Expander.ExpanderTemplateSettings): the resolved display value (the rating shown right now -- pointer-over preview, else committed value, else placeholder), the foreground/background brushes for the current visual state, and the partial-star clip width baked for the foreground "Set" layer. Mirrors the geometry WinUI's RatingControl::UpdateRatingItemsAppearance derives each pass and binds into its two clipped stack panels. Pure factory: no signals are read here -- the caller passes the already-resolved inputs, so this struct is cheap to build per render and never allocates in a hot bind.

The star foreground recolor is INSTANT, never eased: WinUI drives every star visual state with VisualStateManager::GoToState(useTransitions:false) (see UpdateRatingItemsAppearance), so the brush steps the instant the pointer-over preview resolves. We honor that by computing the resting directly from the sweep and leaving the glyph's HoverColor at default (A==0 ⇒ the recorder leaves the color untouched and runs no hover ramp) -- a granular re-render swaps the resting color in one step, no cross-fade.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.RelativeChild
files.Summary: A single child positioned at (, ) within a .

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.RelativePanel
files.Summary: WinUI-style RelativePanel: positions children relative to the panel and to each other. WinUI resolves a constraint graph (AlignLeftWith, RightOf, AlignTopWithPanel, …) into absolute offsets; for this self-contained demo we accept the already-resolved absolute (X, Y) offsets (like ) and render them in a ZStack where each child is wrapped in a BoxEl whose OffsetX/OffsetY translate it into place — enough to show a relative layout result. Children are clipped to the panel bounds.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.ReorderCrossCommit
files.Summary: The ONE cross-list commit callback (plan E5-L3): the app removes the item from from at fromIndex and inserts payload into to at toIndex — one atomic model mutation, both lists re-render from it (signals-first). from is null (and fromIndex −1) when the drag came from a plain BoxEl.Draggable of the shared kind rather than another (a deposit-style insert).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.ReorderList
files.Summary:
Reorder geometry for a one-axis item strip (the WinUI live-reorder model — ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp): given the items' resting main-axis extents and the dragged item's accumulated drag delta (DragEventArgs.TotalDy for a vertical list / TotalDx for a horizontal strip), it computes

  • the PENDING insertion slot under the pointer (midpoint rule: the dragged item's centre crossing a sibling's midpoint claims its slot — the engine analogue of IItemLookupPanel.GetClosestElementInfo + LiveReorderHelper::MovedItems::GetDragOverIndex, ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:984-1063),
  • the dwell-committed TARGET slot — WinUI moves displaced items only after the drag-over index has been stable for the live-reorder timer (200ms list / 300ms grid — LISTVIEW_LIVEREORDER_TIMER / GRIDVIEW_LIVEREORDER_TIMER, ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:50-51), and
  • per-sibling displacement hints (): items between the dragged slot and the target shift by one full dragged-extent (+spacing), the live-reorder "items part to make room" motion (LiveReorderTimerTickHandler → MoveItemsForLiveReorder, ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:2125-2158).

Consumers (ListView/GridView/TabView/TreeView item hosts) wire it to the drag lifecycle: Begin in OnDragStarted, Update(e.TotalDy) in OnDragDelta, Advance(dtMs) from the frame clock; when Advance commits a new target, re-render with the children ORDER projected to it ( — stable keys keep node identity) and a LayoutTransition (Animate) on every item: displaced siblings genuinely change slots, so the engine's FLIP pipeline animates the part-to-make-room motion, and Input.DragController re-anchors the pointer-held visual to its moved slot (no jump). remains for offset-hint consumers — but an authored OffsetX/Y hint must NOT be combined with Animate on the same node: FLIP position tracks own the whole translate channel (CompositeOp.Replace — AnimEngine.ReframePosition) and would stomp the hint at seed and settle. Complete() in OnDragCompleted fires with the collection move; Cancel() in OnDragCanceled drops every hint. All state is grow-only — steady-state reorder (drag at pointer rate) allocates nothing.

TWO-DIMENSIONAL MODE ( / / ) absorbs the GridView live-reorder geometry: the pending slot comes from the dragged tile's accumulated (dx,dy) against the grid (row from the vertical stride, column from the realized column width), with the 300ms grid dwell (GRIDVIEW_LIVEREORDER_TIMER = 300ms — ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:51) re-armed on every drag-over change. A one-slot grid shift can WRAP A ROW (the trailing tile of a row dropping to the head of the next), so the 2-D displacement hint carries BOTH axes. The dwell ticker (), the moved-items projection ( — row-major, identical to 1-D), and are shared with the 1-D path unchanged. = 0 selects the 1-D path; sets it.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.ReorderPayload
files.Summary: The typed payload items carry through the L2 : the owning list, the item's ORIGINAL index there, and the app item (). Any foreign DropTarget accepting the kind reads (track row → sidebar playlist).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.Reorderable
files.Summary:
E5-L3 — drag-reorder for ANY one-axis item list (incl. Virtual.List), composed from the engine layers beneath it (plan Part B E5; user ruling: the Flutter Draggable/DragTarget + react-beautiful-dnd + SwiftUI model, deliberately NOT WinUI's OLE loop): each item is an L2 of , the list body is ONE accepting , the slot math is (WinUI's midpoint rule + live-reorder dwell — ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:984-1063, :50-51), sibling displacement is the engine FLIP pipeline (each wrapped item carries a LayoutTransition; re-rendering the PROJECTED order moves them through their springs), the ghost/lift visuals and the drop-glide are L1 DragController, and edge auto-scroll is engine-level in DragDropContext (WinUI's 100px/150–1500px/s gradient) — this class adds none of those mechanics, only the list semantics.

Usage (inside a component render):

var ro = UseMemo(() => new Reorderable("track") { OnReorder = (f, t) => Move(tracks, f, t) });
ro.Scene = Context.Scene; ro.RequestRender = Context.RequestRerender;
ro.ItemCount = tracks.Count; ro.ItemExtent = 48f;
return ro.List(Virtual.List(tracks.Count, 48f,
    slot => ro.Item(ro.ItemAt(slot), RowFor(tracks[ro.ItemAt(slot)]), key: tracks[ro.ItemAt(slot)].Id)));

Render slots through with STABLE per-item keys: mid-drag the projection moves the dragged item to the dwell-committed slot, the keyed diff keeps node identity, and the FLIP pass animates the displaced siblings (the part-to-make-room motion) while DragController.Move re-anchors the pointer-held visual.

CROSS-LIST: two Reorderables sharing a Kind. The target list accepts the foreign session, shows the insertion line while hovered, and on drop routes the ONE (target's, else the source's) — the source's gesture completion then cancels its local reorder instead of committing (no double mutation).

KEYBOARD lift mode (the react-beautiful-dnd a11y pattern): Space on a focused item lifts it (a pointer-free reorder; the item dims to the L1 drag opacity with the lifted shadow), Up/Down (Left/Right when ) move the insertion slot with NO dwell, Space drops (commits), Escape cancels, and focus leaving the lifted item cancels. All slot moves re-render the projection → FLIP animates them.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.RepeatButton
files.Summary: A button that raises its click repeatedly while held (WinUI RepeatButton): the host's RepeatTicker fires the click once on press, then after an initial delay, then at a steady interval, until release (or until the pointer drags off — WinUI stops the timer when IsPointerOver drops, RepeatButton_Partial.cpp:530-548). Opting in is just = true on a clickable node; the scheduling lives in the host (see RepeatTicker). WinUI defaults: Delay = 500ms, Interval = 33ms — the DP metadata defaults (dxaml\xcp\components\DependencyObject\DependencyProperty.cpp:714-720) — overridable per instance via / (RepeatButton_Partial.cpp:149-182 validates positive). Keyboard mirrors WinUI exactly: a held Space arms the SAME engine timer (RepeatButton_Partial.cpp:212-217 — m_keyboardCausingRepeat; OS key auto-repeat is ignored), a held Enter yields exactly ONE click on its down edge (the ClickMode.Press initialize, RepeatButton_Partial.cpp:29). Style source: controls\dev\CommonStyles\RepeatButton_themeresources.xaml (Default = dark :4-27, Light :52-75) — state storyboards are color-only (lines 100-143), so no press scale (Wave-1 parity pass removed it).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.RepeatLayout
files.Summary: A pluggable layout for — the equivalent of WinUI's Layout object. A readonly struct (no per-call allocation). Stack/Grid/Custom virtualize (only the window is realized); Wrap/Inline are non-virtual (build the whole child list — use for SMALL collections like a nav pane or a toolbar). Measured/LinedFlow/SpanGrid/HorizontalGrid ride the Custom slot (every layout object IS an ; variable-extent ones are — E11-L0's one seam). NOTE: the stateful layouts (Measured/LinedFlow/SpanGrid/Grouped) own realize tables — when the OWNING component re-renders, hoist the layout instance (e.g. UseMemo) instead of calling the factory inline each render.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.RichTextBlock.Options
files.Summary: Per-block options (WinUI DP equivalents). Defaults match the WinUI control: selection enabled (RichTextBlock.cpp:1730), theme selection brush, the established 560px reading measure + 10px paragraph gap.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.RichTextBlock
files.Summary:
WinUI RichTextBlock equivalent: read-only, multi-paragraph rich text laid out as a vertical column of paragraphs. Each paragraph is a — an array of typed inline runs (the WinUI Run/Bold/Hyperlink inline model, RichTextBlock.g.h:63 get_Blocks) that shapes as ONE wrapped flow (rtb-01).

Selection (rtb-02): ON by default — IsTextSelectionEnabled=TRUE by default on CRichTextBlock (RichTextBlock.cpp:1730); mouse drag selects (word on double-click, all on triple), Ctrl+C copies through the clipboard seam (TextSelectionManager.cpp:30-41), the highlight reuses the editor's selection brushes (TextControlSelectionHighlightColor ≡ the system accent, TextSelectionManager.cpp:52-56) and the I-beam shows while selectable. overrides the brush per control (api-04 — TextBlock.cpp:266/330 SelectionHighlightColor). Honest scope: selection is PER PARAGRAPH (the drag clamps to the paragraph it anchored in); WinUI's cross-paragraph selection arrives with the block-collection pass.

Hyperlinks: spans get the WinUI inline-Hyperlink treatment — accent foreground (HyperlinkForeground = AccentTextFillColorPrimary, generic.xaml:1120-1122), underline, the Hand cursor over the span's laid rects and OnClick on release (SetCursor(MouseCursorHand), RichTextBlock.cpp:2995).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.Route
files.Summary: A serializable navigation destination — a route name + an optional argument (e.g. a playlist id).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.ScrollBar
files.Summary:
WinUI ScrollBar. Two surfaces:

  • — the legacy thin PANNING-indicator variant (thumb-only, absolute drag), kept source/behavior-compatible (VerticalSlice check 48 and the gallery drive it); it mirrors the template's VerticalPanningThumb (ScrollBar_themeresources.xaml:714 — Width 2, MinHeight 32, Margin 2,0,2,0, NO hover/press states — the touch indicator).
  • — the full WinUI mouse scrollbar: 12px rail (ScrollBarSize :180), acrylic track (ScrollBarTrackFill = AcrylicInAppFillColorDefaultBrush, :31/:143 both themes), two arrow RepeatButtons (vertical glyphs EDDB up / EDDC down, horizontal EDD9 left / EDDA right, FontSize 8 — :387/:344/:301/:258/:186; pressed arrow scale 0.875 :187; Interval=50 like every template RepeatButton :681-711), track-click PAGE zones (LargeDecrease/LargeIncrease RepeatButtons :704/:710 — first page on press, then 500ms delay + 50ms repeat re-evaluated against the live thumb), and the conscious expand/collapse: collapsed 2px visible thumb ↔ expanded 6px (the 8px/12px thumb minus the 6px transparent stroke trick — ScrollBarVerticalThumbMinWidth 8 :182, ScrollBarThumbStrokeThickness 6 :185, ScrollBarSize 12 :180; both states leave the FILL's far edge inset 3px from the lane edge: collapsed rect [4,12] / expanded [0,12], fill inset 3 per side → [7,9] / [3,9]). Expand begins 400ms after lane hover (ScrollBarExpandBeginTime :188) / collapse begins 500ms after leave (ScrollBarContractBeginTime :189), both 167ms with KeySpline 0,0,0,1 (:587/:543 → ); track + arrows fade 83ms LINEAR (ScrollBarOpacityChangeDuration :174, plain DoubleAnimation :575-584) after the same begin times. While the thumb is DRAGGED a lane exit does NOT start the contract dwell (WinUI OnPointerExited skips UpdateVisualState while IsDragging, ScrollBar_Partial.cpp:548-561) — the leave replays at the drag's release edge. The arrow CELLS are ALWAYS in the grid (rows 0/4, fixed extent = ScrollBarSize :703/:711) — only their OPACITY animates, so the track length and thumb geometry never jump on hover. Thumb fill = ControlStrongFillColorDefault with NO hover/press recolor (:26-28); arrow button background stays SubtleFillColorTransparent in EVERY state (:14 — the :226-248 storyboards recolor only the Arrow foreground); disabled = ControlStrongFillColorDisabled (:29) + root opacity 0.5 (:436) + the mouse thumb itself hidden (ThumbVisual Opacity→0, the VerticalThumbTemplate Disabled storyboard :399-406). IsTabStop = false (:206). Vertical by default; horizontal: true mirrors the HorizontalRoot anatomy on the X axis (:672-693).

Thumb POSITION is compositor-bound (a TransformBind on the position signal): scrolling moves the thumb the same frame with no re-render/relayout and never enters the FLIP pipeline — WinUI's instant Thumb layout. ONLY the conscious cross-axis 2px↔6px expand (and the 83ms chrome fades) animate, after the debounced begin times; that choreography is stepped by a mounted FrameClock ticker at the engine's 16ms-per-frame convention (the UseAnimatedValue precedent), so it is deterministic on the headless FixedFrameTimeSource host. The auto-hiding OVERLAY scrollbar on scroll viewports is engine-drawn (SceneRecorder.EmitScrollbar) with its timing in Animation.ScrollAnimator — this control is the standalone (always-visible) ScrollBar element.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.SelectionModel
files.Summary:
Index/RANGE-based selection state (E11-L3) — WinUI's SelectionModel flattened to one level (our items controls are flat projections; TreeView flattens before selecting). DECOUPLED FROM REALIZATION: selection is a sorted list of disjoint inclusive index ranges, so over 50k rows stores ONE range and realizes nothing — realized items read their state on prepare via (O(log ranges)).

Signals-first: every mutation bumps ; a composing control reads Version.Value in its render so a selection change re-renders exactly that control (which re-skins only its realized window). fires once per mutating call that actually changed state (WinUI SelectionChanged).

Pointer/keyboard semantics are the verified WinUI selector trio (controls\dev\ItemsView): / implement SingleSelector.cpp:25-57, MultipleSelector.cpp:18-92 and ExtendedSelector.cpp:18-83 — anchor+shift ranges, ctrl toggles, focus-follow.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.SelectorBar
files.Summary: A WinUI SelectorBar: a segmented horizontal row of text (optionally icon+text) items. The selected item is marked by a SHORT CENTERED accent pill flush with the item bottom (not a full-width underline, not bold); text stays at rest for every item and DIMS on hover/press — backgrounds are transparent in every state (SelectorBar_themeresources.xaml:21-25). Stateless — the caller owns selected and reacts to onSelect. Keyboard: ONE tab stop (the selected item — TabNavigation=Once, SelectorBar.xaml:13); Left/Right/ Home/End move focus item-to-item and SELECTION FOLLOWS FOCUS (SelectorBarTests.cs:63-66); focus entering a bar without a selection auto-selects (SelectorBar.cpp:98-126). Per-part restyling goes through the optional parts (see for the contract).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.SelectorVisual
files.Summary:
The selector-VISUAL presets — the user-pickable item-chrome skins the premiere exposes (the WinUI "ListView's accent bar" the user likes, GridView's corner check, ItemContainer's accent ring, …) as a single DATA enum so any layout × any selection mode can wear any selector (no WinUI-style capability cliffs):

  • — the WinUI ListViewItem SelectionIndicator (the 3×16 accent bar at the row's left edge over a subtle backplate); the ListView preset's default.
  • — the WinUI GridViewItem dual-border + top-right corner check; the GridView preset's default.
  • — a documented SUPERSET (no exact WinUI analogue): the AccentPill row plate WITHOUT the left pill, so the whole row fills with the accent-subtle selected highlight (full-bleed selected backplate).
  • — the WinUI ItemContainer chrome (3px accent ring + 1px inner stroke + checkbox).
  • — no selection chrome at all (a bare recyclable container) for app-drawn selection.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.SelectorVisuals
files.Summary:
The presets as -shaped builders: each returns the WinUI item chrome for one preset around the engine's ONE selection + keyboard substrate, signature-identical to the ItemContainerFactory delegate (ItemsView.cs:52-54) so a preset is just ContainerFactory = SelectorVisuals.<Name>.

Each builder is PLAIN/recyclable — a keyed tree with NO Component / no reactive bind (TransformBind/OpacityBind/FillBind/WidthBind/HeightBind/OnRealized would break TreeReconciler.IsRecyclable, Reconciler.cs:748-749); Animate (a ) is allowed because it is declarative. Per-item chrome SKIN goes through the ContainerFactory/SelectorVisual seam; per-item VARIATION goes through the value seam (fill/fg/opacity/corner/padding/glyph as values, applied during construction — shape-stable, 0-alloc, CI-enforced; docs/guide/control-fidelity.md §6). WinUI chrome values are cited inline; they are re-verified against the live ListViewBaseItemChrome.cpp metrics (s_selectionIndicatorSize {3,16}, s_selectionIndicatorHeightShrinkage 6, s_selectionIndicatorMargin.left 4, s_backplateMargin {4,2}, s_multiSelectRoundedSquareInlineMargin 14, s_multiSelectRoundedContentOffset 28, s_multiSelectSquareOverlayMargin {0,2,2,0}) and the per-state brush ramps in {ListView,GridView,ItemContainer}_themeresources.xaml.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.Slider.Options
files.Summary: Extended slider configuration: range, step snapping, ticks, orientation, header, keyboard step sizes and the thumb value tooltip ("disambiguation UI").

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.Slider.TickPlacement
files.Summary: Where tick marks render — WinUI TickPlacement (visibility mapping: Slider_Partial.cpp:2248-2303). WinUI's default is (marks inside the rail, SliderInlineTickBarFill).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.Slider
files.Summary:
A WinUI 3 Slider. Geometry (Slider_themeresources.xaml): a 4px rail (SliderTrackFill = ControlStrongFillColorDefault, radius 2) with an accent value-fill, and the Fluent thumb — a STATIC 22px ring (the 18px Thumb part + its Border's −2 margin, lines 169-172 + 198) of ControlSolidFillColorDefault with a 1px ControlElevationBorder, holding the only animated part: the 12px accent inner dot whose storyboards scale it 0.86 at rest / 1.167 on hover / 0.71 on press / 1.167 disabled (lines 204-254). Value-fill and inner dot recolor with the CONTROL-wide PointerOver/Pressed states (AccentFillColorDefault → Secondary → Tertiary; lines 14-17, 24-27).

  • / — the lightweight 0..1 scrub primitives (media seek/volume). Keyboard, focus visuals and the optional header are included; the value tooltip is not (see ).
  • — the full WinUI-parity control over : arbitrary range, step snapping, ticks (incl. inline), Header, SmallChange/LargeChange keyboard, and the IsThumbToolTipEnabled "disambiguation UI" value tooltip with a ThumbToolTipValueConverter-equivalent formatter.

Keyboard (KeyPress::Slider::KeyDown, SliderKeyProcess.h:28-71): Left/Down decrease and Right/Up increase by SmallChange, Home/End jump to Minimum/Maximum; PageUp/PageDown move by LargeChange (not in XAML's KeyPress::Slider — kept per the parity contract, riding the same Slider::Step math, Slider_Partial.cpp:1713-1783). RTL note: WinUI inverts Left/Right by (FlowDirection==RightToLeft) ^ IsDirectionReversed (SliderKeyProcess.h:26); the engine has no FlowDirection yet — the LTR mapping ships now, the inversion lands with the Wave-6 RTL sweep.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.SplitButton
files.Summary:
A WinUI 3 SplitButton: a primary action button joined to a secondary dropdown half, sharing one rounded chrome with a 1px divider between them. The two halves are independently interactive — the primary half runs (the Click event / OnClickPrimary), the secondary half opens a anchored below the whole control (OnClickSecondary → OpenFlyout). Each half carries its own hover/press fill + foreground ramp; the inner corner where each half meets the divider is squared so the pair reads as one control (WinUI PrimaryButtonBorder 4,0,0,4 / SecondaryButtonBorder 0,4,4,0).

Source of truth: microsoft-ui-xaml/controls/dev/SplitButton/SplitButton.xaml (+ _themeresources.xaml, SplitButton.cpp). Tokens, sizes, padding, corners and the state matrix are 1:1 with that template.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.SplitView
files.Summary:
A WinUI SplitView: a side pane and a flexible content area, composed per SplitViewDisplayMode × PanePlacement × IsPaneOpen (the WinUI visual-state table, SplitView_Partial.cpp:34-59):

<ul><li><b>Overlay</b> (the WinUI default) — the open pane floats over the content without reflow (PaneRoot
Canvas.ZIndex=1 over a ColumnSpan=2 ContentRoot, SplitView_themeresources.xaml:700, :718) and light-dismisses on
content click / Escape / a host resize, with the WinUI 0.35s/0.12s slide.</li><li><b>Inline</b> — the open pane reserves <code>paneWidth</code> of layout; the content pre-slides in sync
(ContentTransform, SplitView_themeresources.xaml:345-348) over the 0.2s/0.1s inline curves.</li><li><b>CompactOverlay / CompactInline</b> — closed leaves a <code>compactPaneLength</code> (48) rail: the SAME pane,
clipped to the rail (a clip-reveal, never a resize — core SplitView.cpp:343-377).</li></ul>

State is caller-owned (<code class="paramref">isPaneOpen</code> is a prop, the WinUI <code>IsPaneOpen</code> DP): wire
<code class="paramref">setIsPaneOpen</code> so light dismiss can write the close back. PaneOpening/PaneOpened/
PaneClosing(cancelable on light dismiss)/PaneClosed mirror the WinUI events (XCPTypesAutoGen SplitView.cs:82-96).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.SplitViewDisplayMode
files.Summary: WinUI SplitViewDisplayMode (XCPTypesAutoGen Modules/Controls/SplitView/SplitView.cs:19-25). Overlay/CompactOverlay float the open pane ABOVE the content (no reflow) and are light-dismissible; Inline/CompactInline reserve layout width for it. Compact modes keep a CompactPaneLength rail visible while closed (the pane is CLIPPED to the rail, never resized — core SplitView.cpp:343-377 template settings).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.SplitViewPaneClosingArgs
files.Summary: WinUI SplitViewPaneClosingEventArgs (XCPTypesAutoGen SplitView.cs:31-39): set to keep the pane open. Honored on LIGHT-DISMISS closes only (core SplitView.cpp:212-244 TryCloseLightDismissiblePane → CSplitViewPaneClosingExecutor:27-38); an app-initiated close raises the event but ignores Cancel (core SplitView.cpp:432-456).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.SplitViewPanePlacement
files.Summary: WinUI SplitViewPanePlacement (XCPTypesAutoGen SplitView.cs:11-15).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.StateBrush
files.Summary: Reusable, WinUI-accurate discrete-state motion presets for control sub-elements — the checkmarks, dots and glyphs that appear/disappear on a logical state flip (checked, selected, expanded…). These ride the engine's existing enter/exit lifecycle: attach one as on a node the reconciler INSERTS on enter and REMOVES on exit, and it scales+fades in (on insert) / out (orphaned, then reclaimed when the spring settles, on removal). The motion is property-level and signals-native — the state IS the element's presence and the animation falls out of the reconcile diff — so there is no per-control animation code and no VisualStateManager: the opposite of WinUI's stringly-typed GoToState storyboards, and a strict superset in composability. Springs (velocity-carrying, interruptible) are the default; durations map to the Fluent motion tokens.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.SwipeAction
files.Summary: One swipe action (WinUI SwipeItem — SwipeControl.idl:69-91): an icon glyph, a label, an optional custom background Color (SwipeItem.Background — flips the content to on-accent text), the callback (the Invoked event / Command) and .

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.SwipeBehaviorOnInvoked
files.Summary: WinUI SwipeBehaviorOnInvoked (SwipeControl.idl:11-16). Auto and Close both close the control after the invoke (SwipeItem::InvokeSwipe closes on Close OR Auto — SwipeItem.cpp:40-45); RemainOpen keeps it revealed.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.SwipeControl
files.Summary: A WinUI SwipeControl (controls\dev\SwipeControl): drag the content sideways to reveal action items underneath. The content pans with the pointer; releasing past min(items width, 100px) rests it open (c_ThresholdValue — SwipeControl.cpp:21, ConfigurePositionInertiaRestingValues :937-1005), otherwise it springs closed; an already-open side closes on ANY inward movement (UpdateThresholdReached, :1626-1645). Items are invokable (Invoked + BehaviorOnInvoked); Execute mode stretches one item across the control and auto-invokes it on open. Esc and a tap on the content dismiss an open swipe (AttachDismissingHandlers, SwipeControl.h:74-76). Horizontal (Left/Right) items only — WinUI's vertical Top/Bottom axis is not offered on this surface, so its "both axes populated" rejection (ThrowIfHasVerticalAndHorizontalContent, SwipeControl.cpp:1648-1676) cannot arise. Deliberate superset: WinUI only swipes on touch/pen (VisualInteractionSource); ours pans with the mouse too.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.SwipeMode
files.Summary: WinUI SwipeMode (SwipeControl.idl:20-24): Reveal = resting tappable action buttons; Execute = ONE item stretched to the control width that auto-invokes when the swipe opens past the threshold.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.TabView
files.Summary: A WinUI TabView (controls\dev\TabView): a horizontal strip of tab headers atop the selected tab's content. The control OWNS its live tab collection (the WinUI TabItems vector, TabView.idl:135), seeded once from / at mount: the close button / middle-click / Ctrl+F4 remove the tab (after raising ) with WinUI's reselect-at-the-removed-index rule (TabView.cpp:786-812); the add button appends what returns; pointer drag reorders tabs (, default true). Equal width mode clamps tabs to 100–240px of the measured strip; overflowing tabs scroll behind 32×24 repeat scroll buttons (±50px, disabled at the extremes) and the selected tab is auto-scrolled into view. The selected header uses the SolidBackgroundFillColorTertiary surface and fuses flush with the content area (the strip's 1px CardStroke baseline is per-tab and omitted under the selected header). Per-part restyling goes through (see for the contract).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.TabViewCloseButtonOverlayMode
files.Summary: WinUI TabViewCloseButtonOverlayMode (TabView.idl:15-20). Auto/Always show the close button on every closable tab; OnPointerOver shows it only while the tab is selected or hovered (TabViewItem.cpp:251-282).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.TabViewItem
files.Summary: One tab (WinUI TabViewItem, TabView.idl:205-230): a Header string, an optional leading icon glyph (the IconSource slot, idl:214-215), per-tab IsClosable (default true, idl:217-219), and a Content factory rendered in the content area while this tab is selected.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.TabViewWidthMode
files.Summary: WinUI TabViewWidthMode (TabView.idl:6-11) — how tab headers size. Default Equal: every tab gets clamp(available/count, 100, 240) (TabView.cpp:1180-1228; TabViewItemMinWidth/MaxWidth, TabView_themeresources.xaml:243-244). SizeToContent: intrinsic. Compact: unselected tabs collapse to icon-only (TabViewItem.cpp:284-295; TabView.xaml:484-490).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.TeachingTip.CloseReason
files.Summary: WinUI TeachingTipCloseReason — surfaced on Closing/Closed.

files.Summary: WinUI TeachingTipClosingEventArgs (synchronous Cancel slice; async Deferral is deep-deferred).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.TeachingTip
files.Summary:
WinUI TeachingTip (controls/dev/TeachingTip/TeachingTip.xaml + _themeresources + .cpp/.idl): an anchored (targeted) or window-relative (untargeted) non-modal callout that teaches/highlights a feature. The body is a SOLID surface — SolidBackgroundFillColorTertiary background, SurfaceStrokeColorDefault 1px border, OverlayCornerRadius (8), an elevation shadow, and the 1px TeachingTipTopHighlightBrush top highlight (#0DFFFFFF dark / #99FFFFFF light, TeachingTip_themeresources.xaml:6/:28) — with (top→bottom) an optional hero image, an icon + title + subtitle row, the main content, and a footer button panel (Action + Close). A targeted tip grows a 16×8 tail/beak that points at the target; an untargeted tip has no tail. NOT light-dismiss by default (IsLightDismissEnabled=false): only the close button, Escape, or a programmatic close dismisses it.

The FULL 13-placement matrix (TeachingTip.xaml PlacementStates :115-264) is honored — Top/Bottom/Left/Right, the 8 corner variants, and Center — each mapping to the matching FlyoutPositioner placement with the tail pinned to the joined edge per the state's alignment setters (e.g. TopRight → tail at the LEFT of the bottom edge, :160-170). nudges the tip away from the target (TeachingTip.cpp:480/:588 offset); (default true, the WinUI idl default) opts the popup out of the window bounds via the E4 windowed-popup seam when false.

1:1 tokens/sizes (TeachingTip_themeresources.xaml, unitless = epx): • MinWidth 320, MaxWidth 336, MinHeight 40, MaxHeight 520 (TeachingTipMin/MaxWidth/Height). • Content margin 12 on all sides (TeachingTipContentMargin); button panel top margin 0,12,0,0 (TeachingTipButtonPanelMargin); both-buttons split — Action 0,12,4,0 (Left), Close 4,12,0,0 (Right). • MainContent present-margin 0,12,0,0 (gap below the title block when content exists); icon→title gap 12 (TeachingTipIconPresenterMarginWithIcon); title-stack right margin 28 when the header (alternate) close button shows. • Title FontSize 16 SemiBold; Subtitle FontSize 14; body content FontSize 14 (Foreground = TextPrimary). • Alternate (header) close button: 40×40 (TeachingTipAlternateCloseButtonSize), E711 glyph at 16px, SubtleFill ramp, ControlCornerRadius. The footer ActionButton's DEFAULT style is the STANDARD button (TeachingTip.xaml:10 ActionButtonStyle = DefaultButtonStyle) — set for the AccentButtonStyle the gallery samples use.

Open/close motion (host-driven, PopupChrome.TeachingTip): expand scale → 1 over 300ms cubic-bezier(0.1,0.9,0.2,1); contract scale → 20/Width over 200ms cubic-bezier(0.7,0,1,0.5) (TeachingTip.cpp:1660-1712; .h:234-235 + :304-307 control points) — both at full opacity (the storyboards carry no opacity keyframes).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.TextBlocks

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.TextBox
files.Summary: WinUI TextBox: a text field built on , optionally with a header label stacked above and a description row below (the WinUI template's HeaderContentPresenter / DescriptionPresenter, TextBox_themeresources.xaml:335/340). Header-less it is just the bare field. EditableText already supplies the bordered surface (fill, gradient ControlElevationBorder, focus affordance, 4px corners) — TextBox wraps no second border. Single-line shows the DeleteButton (✕ E894 while focused ∧ non-empty); multi-line (acceptsReturn) hides it (WinUI: the DeleteButton is suppressed when TextWrapping/AcceptsReturn make the box multi-line).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.TextChangeReason
files.Summary: Why the field's text changed — the WinUI AutoSuggestionBoxTextChangeReason shape, surfaced through so AutoSuggestBox/ComboBox can split user edits from programmatic writes.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.TimePicker
files.Summary: A WinUI TimePicker: a single Button FACE split into Hour | Minute | AM/PM text columns (equal star widths, TimePicker_themeresources.xaml:234-239) separated by 1px vertical spacers, and on tap opens ONE TimePickerFlyout presenter via the service (the whole face is a single FlyoutButton, TimePicker_themeresources.xaml:232). The popup is placed so the centered 40px accent highlight band overlays the face (DateTimePickerFlyoutHelper.cpp:39-71) and is width-matched to the face's realized width (TimePickerFlyout_Partial.cpp:196-200). It hosts side-by-side LOOPING selector columns (item height 40) — the shared — under the highlight band, with a 41px Accept(E8FB) / Dismiss(E711) bar. is nullable (null shows the hour/minute/AM placeholders, the WinUI HasNoTime state); selects the 12/24-hour clock and coarsens the minute column.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.TitleBar
files.Summary:
The WinUI 3 TitleBar control (WinAppSDK 1.7, microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\TitleBar) over a custom frame (): back + pane-toggle buttons (40w, 16px glyphs), a 16×16 app-identity icon, 12px Caption title/subtitle, a centered content column (the gallery's AutoSuggestBox), and — unlike WinUI, which reserves space for SHELL-drawn caption buttons — three ENGINE-drawn min/max/close s (46w, full bar height, close = shell red) so the whole bar is one GPU-rendered surface.

Non-client plumbing: the control captures its part handles (OnRealized) and, in a layout effect (after layout, before paint — push-on-relayout only, never per frame), reports s through : interactive islands (back/pane/content) FIRST as , then the three button rects (→ HTMIN/HTMAX/HTCLOSE — the Win11 snap-layouts flyout requires HTMAXBUTTON), then the whole bar as the catch-all drag band (first match wins in WM_NCHITTEST). Window activation/placement changes arrive via the host-bumped signal: deactivation dims title→tertiary, icon/content→50% opacity, caption glyphs→disabled (the WinUI Deactivated visual state); maximize re-glyphs max↔restore.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.ToggleButton
files.Summary: A two/three-state toggle (shuffle/repeat/follow). Accent-filled when on — WinUI ToggleButton's checked state (AccentFillColorDefault bg + TextOnAccent fg + AccentControlElevationBorder); the unchecked AND indeterminate states are a standard control fill. Controlled: pass the state and a toggle/cycle callback. Style source: controls\dev\CommonStyles\ToggleButton_themeresources.xaml (Default = dark :4-61, Light :120-177; DefaultToggleButtonStyle :180-363). The full WinUI matrix is wired: {Unchecked, Checked, Indeterminate} × {Normal, PointerOver, Pressed, Disabled} for fill, foreground and border, verified in both themes. State flips (checked↔unchecked) cross-fade over the WinUI BrushTransition 83ms (ContentPresenter.BackgroundTransition, ToggleButton_themeresources.xaml:199-201) — the E3 primitive. No scale animation: WinUI state storyboards are color-only (lines 202-357).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.ToggleSplitButton
files.Summary:
A WinUI ToggleSplitButton (SplitButton + checked state). Like it is one joined chrome with two independently-clickable halves — a primary half on the left and a dropdown half on the right separated by a 1px divider — but the primary half toggles on/off instead of running a one-shot action: clicking it (or Enter/Space activation) flips and raises ; the dropdown half opens a . WinUI derives ToggleSplitButton from SplitButton and they share one ControlTemplate; the checked visual-states are driven by InternalIsChecked() (SplitButton.cpp UpdateVisualStates) — so this control is the SplitButton chrome with the accent Checked* token ramps folded in.

When checked, both halves fill accent (SplitButtonBackgroundChecked = AccentFillColorDefault), the foreground is TextOnAccent, the outer border is AccentControlElevationBorder, and the inner divider switches to the on-accent divider stroke. Open/close goes through the overlay popup service (): re-click toggles, Escape / click-outside light-dismiss, focus captured on open + restored on close (host-wired), FocusTrap roves the menu, and ExpandCollapse Collapsed↔Expanded is raised by the overlay lifecycle. Keyboard: Enter/Space toggle the primary (the engine's focused-clickable activation), Down opens the flyout. gates all interaction via the engine and folds the chrome to the disabled tokens. The checked state is a caller so a page can read/display it; pass to observe flips.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.ToggleSwitch
files.Summary:
A WinUI ToggleSwitch — geometry/colors/motion from the shipped lifted style (microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\CommonStyles\ToggleSwitch_themeresources.xaml, "the template"): a 40×20 pill track (OuterBorder/SwitchKnobBounds, template:507-508) + a knob inside a 20×20 positioning host (SwitchKnob grid, template:509) that translates 0→20 on toggle (KnobTranslateTransform To=20, template:445). The knob is 12×12 at rest (template:510/515 + Normal storyboard :231-242), grows to 14×14 on hover (:268-279), and STRETCHES to 17×14 pinned 3px from the near edge while pressed (size :311-322; the Pressed VisualState.Setters pin SwitchKnobOn right / SwitchKnobOff left with a 3px margin, :284-287). All knob size/anchor changes tween 83ms ControlFasterAnimationDuration with the ControlFastOutSlowInKeySpline (0,0,0,1) (Common_themeresources_any.xaml:602/606 — ); the disabled return is 250ms ControlNormalAnimationDuration (:357-368); knob travel is WinUI's RepositionThemeAnimation (:418-439), mapped per the repo motion canon to = 167ms (Dsl/Motion.cs — the PVL reposition timing is system data, not in the XAML).

Behavior follows dxaml\xcp\dxaml\lib\ToggleSwitch_Partial.cpp: tap toggles (TapHandler :874-905); DRAG-TO-TOGGLE — the knob follows the pointer's horizontal delta clamped to the travel (DragDeltaHandler/MoveDelta :823-841/:579-589, SetTranslations clamp :455-457) and on release toggles iff the knob crossed half the travel range (MoveCompleted :591-619); pointer loss mid-drag cleans up without toggling (OnPointerCaptureLost :728-746). Space toggles via the engine's Space-on-keyup activation → OnClick (HandlesKey = Space/GamepadA only, :1002-1007); arrow/Home/End keys toggle directionally (ToggleSwitchKeyProcess.h:52-71 — WinUI acts on KeyUp; the engine routes OnKeyDown, a known timing-only deviation). Controlled — the caller owns isOn.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.ToolTip
files.Summary:
A WinUI ToolTip (framework ToolTip + ToolTipService): wraps a target element and surfaces a small text bubble anchored above it (or at the pointer in ). 1:1 with the framework's automatic-tooltip behavior:

  • Hover trigger — pointer-over the target opens the bubble after the initial show delay (SPI_GETMOUSEHOVERTIME 400ms × 2 = 800ms; a re-show within = 200ms uses the reshow delay — see for why that is 400ms, not the spec'd 600ms). Pointer-leave cancels a PENDING open immediately (ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1435-1442); an OPEN bubble is kept and closed by the safe-zone monitor instead — see the safe-zone item below.
  • Safe zone — an open bubble does NOT close the instant the pointer leaves the target: WinUI keeps it while the pointer is inside owner ∪ tooltip (∪ their convex hull) and a 1s check timer closes it once outside (ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1433-1453 owner-exit only records the owner; cpp:349-381 OnSafeZoneCheck; cpp:1060-1098 IsToolTipInSafeZone; .h:22 s_safeZoneCheckTimerDuration = 1s). The engine surfaces no global pointer position, so the hull is approximated by the same 1s cadence: leaving owner ∪ bubble arms a grace; re-entering either cancels it; the grace elapsing closes the bubble.
  • Keyboard trigger — KEYBOARD focus landing inside the target opens the bubble after 800ms (×2, normal AND reshow: ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1777-1779), exactly WinUI's OnOwnerGotFocus gate (ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1648-1664: only FocusState::Keyboard shows the tooltip — pointer focus does not). Focus leaving closes it (cpp:1696-1706 OnOwnerLostFocus → OnOwnerLeaveInternal).
  • Press dismiss — a pointer press over the target closes an open (or pending) bubble, and it stays closed until the pointer LEAVES and re-enters (classic tooltip behavior; WinUI 3's ToolTipService itself registers no PointerPressed handler — it closes via safe-zone exit monitoring, ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1437-1453 — but cannot re-open a dismissed owner until a real leave + re-enter either, cpp:725-737 CancelAutomaticToolTip).
  • Auto-dismiss — an open bubble closes itself after (SPI_GETMESSAGEDURATION default 5s), and the dismissal LATCHES like press dismiss: WinUI's only show trigger is PointerEntered (ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:1395-1418), so in-place hover moves can never re-open a timed-out tooltip — it takes a real leave + re-enter.
  • No click trigger — the wrapper adds NO OnClick (ToolTipService registers none, ToolTipService_Partial.cpp:176-220), so a tooltipped element is never a tab stop by itself.

Chrome matches ToolTip_themeresources.xaml: AcrylicInAppFillColorDefault fill, 1px SurfaceStrokeColorFlyout stroke, ControlCornerRadius (4px) corners, the light tooltip elevation (), 9,6,9,8 padding, 12px TextFillColorPrimary text, MaxWidth 320, TextWrapping=Wrap. Open/close are the WinUI FadeIn/FadeOutThemeAnimation fades (167ms — in the overlay host). The bubble does NOT trap focus and does NOT light-dismiss on outside click () — dismissal is owned by hover-leave/focus-leave/press + the auto-dismiss timer, exactly like ToolTipService.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.ToolTipPlacementMode
files.Summary: How the tooltip bubble is positioned — WinUI PlacementMode (the two modes ToolTipService actually drives): = centered above the TARGET, flipping on a collision (the WinUI default, ToolTip_Partial.cpp:1119-1122 "Fall back to the default - PlacementMode_Top"), the target rect inflated by the input-mode offset — 20px mouse / 12px keyboard (); = at the last POINTER position, offset 11px below the cursor (ToolTip_Partial.h:56 m_mousePlacementVerticalOffset = 11; ToolTip_Partial.cpp:976-984 "align ToolTip with the bottom left corner of mouse bounding rectangle").

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.TreeNode
files.Summary: One node in a : a label plus zero or more child nodes. gates interaction (disabled rows dim to 0.3); custom row content comes from the tree's ItemTemplate.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.Tween
files.Summary: The typed computed-"TemplateSettings" convention — the antidote to WinUI's stringly TemplateSettings bound into storyboards. A control declares a local readonly record struct <Control>TemplateSettings(...) of typed values (rotations, heights, clip rects, open/close animation positions), computes it ONCE per state change (a pure factory called in render, or a UseMemo/OnRealized effect that reads the control's own signals), and feeds the fields into the engine's existing channels: static props, TransformBind/OpacityBind/WidthBind/ HeightBind, AnimEngine keyframe seeds, or the clip-rect channel (AnimChannel.ClipL/T/R/B). Never rebuild the record inside a hot bind/effect body — compute it in the memo/factory. is the reference; the same shape serves CommandBarFlyout, NavigationView, Progress*, TeachingTip, TabView, TreeView, … These small helpers are the shared interpolation math those records use.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.VariableSizedWrapGrid
files.Summary: A WinUI VariableSizedWrapGrid: a grid that wraps tiles of varying sizes. This is a self-contained, non-virtualized demo — fixed-base cell tiles where some span multiple cells. Tiles are greedily packed left-to-right into rows that hold columns cells (by ColSpan sum); a tile that would overflow the current row starts a new one. Cells are contiguous (no inter-cell gap) — an N-span tile is exactly N*cell — matching the WinUI base panel, which is an unstyled layout host (no per-tile chrome). A faint fill is kept only for visibility.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.Viewbox
files.Summary: A WinUI Viewbox: scales its single child by a composited transform (no relayout). WinUI computes the scale from a layout measure pass; this engine has no measure-override seam on , so the scale is supplied either as an explicit factor (the scale overload) or computed from a known content size and target box via / logic (the sized overload). The child is wrapped in a centered carrying a uniform /.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.ViewboxStretch
files.Summary: How a scales its child to the available box. Mirrors WinUI Stretch (default ).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.ViewboxStretchDirection
files.Summary: Constrains a scale direction. Mirrors WinUI StretchDirection (default ).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.Virtual
files.Summary: Fluent factories for virtualized collections. All recycle over the slab free-list and keep a 0-alloc in-window scroll. takes any you implement. The returned record lives in FluentGpu.Reconciler (the reconciler diffs it directly).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Controls.WrapTile
files.Summary: One tile in a : a label plus how many base cells it spans horizontally (ColSpan) and vertically (RowSpan).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Dsl.BoxEl
files.Summary: A box: container/layout node and/or a filled, optionally-clickable surface (VStack/HStack/Button/Box).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Dsl.Element
files.Summary: Immutable description of a UI node (the "virtual DOM"). Cheap to build, never touches the scene directly.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Dsl.Elevation
files.Summary: Fluent elevation presets — the engine's ANALYTIC drop shadows standing in for WinUI's ThemeShadow Z-depth model (we own every pixel; documented visual-equivalent to the DComp shadow). WinUI elevates popups by Z translation — base depth 32 + 8 per nested tier (ElevationHelper.cpp:19-21 s_elevationBaseDepth/s_elevationIterativeDepth) — and the design maps the popup bands to three depth CLASSES: ToolTip (lightest, depth-16 class) < Flyout/Menu (depth-32 class) < Dialog (depth-128 class). The preset blur/offset/alpha values keep the engine's existing visual scale; the CLASS a control uses is the contract: ToolTip → ; Flyout/MenuFlyout/CommandBarFlyout/TeachingTip → ; ContentDialog → . Use via .Elevate(Elevation.Card) (Modifiers) or Ui.Card(...) which carries by default.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Dsl.GridEl
files.Summary: A CSS-Grid container: column tracks (Pixel/Star/Auto) + gaps; children auto-flow row-major into cells. Rows take (or the tallest cell when NaN). True tracks — every cell in a column shares its width (unlike nested-flex faking). Width-aware: resolves inside a ScrollView. (layout.md §7.)

files.Title: FluentGpu.Dsl.IconGlyphs
files.Summary: Segoe Fluent glyph codepoints shared across LAYERS (a control and the engine recorder), so every consumer renders literally the same icon. Control-local glyphs stay at their call sites (or Controls.Icons); promote one here only when a layer below Controls needs it — today the scrollbar arrows, drawn both by the ScrollBar/AnnotatedScrollBar templates and by the engine's overlay scrollbar (SceneRecorder.EmitScrollbar).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Dsl.ImageEl
files.Summary: An async image (album art): shows until the decode lands, then the bitmap. Decode is off-thread + cached + residency-pinned while on screen (see ImageCache).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Dsl.Modifiers
files.Summary: Fluent per-element style overrides (React/Compose/WinUI-style): Button("x", onClick).Background(c).Rounded(8). Each returns a record with-copy, so any default style is overrideable inline without forking the control.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Dsl.Motion
files.Summary: The real Fluent 2 / WinUI motion durations + entrance metrics (durations only — the easing curves live in FluentGpu.Foundation.Easing, selected by the Hooks-layer motion helpers, since Dsl does not reference Animation). Canonical WinUI names (the motion audit's correction): ControlFaster = 83ms (BrushTransition, hover/press), ControlFast = 167ms (ControlFastAnimationDuration — knob travel, settle), ControlNormal = 250ms (ControlNormalAnimationDuration — reposition / pane motion).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Dsl.PolylineStrokeEl
files.Summary: A text run. FontFamily selects the face — a system family name ("Segoe UI", "Segoe Fluent Icons") or a custom file as "path/to.ttf#Family Name" (the WinUI syntax). Null = the theme body font. Combine with an icon-font family + a private-use glyph to render icons (see Ui.Icon).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Dsl.Radii
files.Summary: The WinUI Fluent corner-radius ramp (ControlCornerRadius / OverlayCornerRadius + the SelectorBar pill).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Dsl.ScrollEl
files.Summary: A clipping, scrolling viewport over a single (possibly oversized) content child. Scroll is layout-free (layout.md §6): layout arranges the content at the content-box origin and publishes ContentSize; the -ScrollOffset translation is the content's LocalTransform, written by Input on wheel/drag.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Dsl.Spacing
files.Summary: The WinUI Gallery spacing rhythm (page gutters, card spacing, internal padding, stack gaps).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Dsl.SpanTextEl
files.Summary: A rich-text PARAGRAPH of typed inline runs (rtb-01 — the WinUI TextBlock.Inlines/RichTextBlock paragraph model: Run/Bold/Hyperlink): the spans concatenate and shape as ONE flow (one wrap pass — a styled word never re-flows independently of its sentence). Per-span weight/size/family/color/underline/strikethrough overlay the base style here; a span with OnClick is a HYPERLINK — the engine resolves the Hand cursor over its laid rects and fires the action on click (RichTextBlock.cpp:2995 / TextBlock.cpp:3488 SetCursor(MouseCursorHand)); accent foreground + underline are the CALLER's styling (HyperlinkForeground, generic.xaml:1120-1122). No per-span italic: the shaper has no style axis yet (faces resolve by family+weight only).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Dsl.TemplateParts
files.Summary:
Lightweight template-part styling — the FluentGpu equivalent of CSS ::part / WinUI lightweight styling, signals-native. A control names its internal template parts (public const string PartHeader = "Header"; …) and routes each built part element through ; app code layers ANY element props onto any part with a with expression:

Parts = new()
{
    [Expander.PartHeader] = b => b with
    {
        StickyTop = 8f,                                    // CSS position:sticky, top: 8px
        OnPinned  = p => stuck.Value = p,                  // the :stuck observable
        Fill = stuck.Value ? Tok.FillSolidBase : b.Fill,   // restyle ANYTHING off the signal
        BrushTransitionMs = Motion.ControlFast,            // …and the swap cross-fades
    },
}

Contract (apply order): control defaults → part modifier → the control RE-ASSERTS its mechanics-critical props (click handlers, reflow specs, ref captures — chained via , never clobbered), so a modifier can restyle everything but break nothing. Modifier rules: a PURE with-copy of its input; reading a signal inside one subscribes the OWNING CONTROL (modifiers run inside its Render — that's the granular :stuck restyle loop); never WRITE a signal in a modifier; no per-frame-hot reads (use FillBind/TransformBind for those); type-preserving (a modifier that changes the record type is ignored — parts style, content SLOTS like Content/HeaderContent restructure); avoid reshaping Children (structural reconcile).

List-UNIFORM (content-independent) item-chrome modifiers are cached apply-once via the parts epoch (); per-item CONTENT differences use the PartDelta value seam (SelectorVisuals.cs), never a per-item modifier in a recycled scroll path.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Dsl.TextTiers
files.Summary: Color-tier modifiers for any TextEl: Ui.Body("x").Secondary().

files.Title: FluentGpu.Dsl.Theme
files.Summary: Back-compat facade over the semantic token table (). The flat names here mirror what existing call sites use; each forwards to the active , so a theme swap () re-themes them too. New code should prefer / directly. The host still injects the OS accent + Mica background through the / setters.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Dsl.Tok
files.Summary: The active design-token table + the theme switch. Author UI reads tokens via the static getters (e.g. Tok.FillCardDefault); they resolve against at zero per-read cost. re-themes the whole app with one pointer write. The OS accent + Mica window background are mutable overrides on top of the set, so they survive a theme swap and let the host inject the live system accent at startup.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Dsl.TokenSet
files.Summary: An immutable, baked palette of every semantic Fluent brush for one theme (mirrors WinUI's *_themeresources). Built once per theme; never mutated. Read through , which swaps the active set with a single pointer write.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Dsl.Ui
files.Summary: Fluent C# markup. using static FluentGpu.Dsl.Ui; to author trees as expressions.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.AcrylicSpec
files.Summary: A per-node acrylic (frosted glass): the engine samples the canvas behind the node, resolves transparent backdrop through , blurs it (), then applies WinUI's luminosity/tint recipe. Realized by the PushLayer/PopLayer backdrop subsystem.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.Affine2D
files.Summary: 2x3 affine (local→parent). 2.5D/perspective out of scope (per spec).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.ArcSpec
files.Summary: A circular-arc stroke (WinUI ProgressRing — a trimmed, round-capped ring). The arc is centred in the node's box with radius (min(W,H)-Thickness)/2, a -wide stroke, swept from for degrees (0° = 12 o'clock, clockwise). The recorder emits a DrawArc; the GPU draws the SDF arc with round caps. A full ring is SweepDeg = 360.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.ArenaAllocator
files.Summary: Per-frame bump allocator. O(1) . Spans are invalidated by Reset (and by a grow).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.AutomationRole
files.Summary: The semantic role of a node, set by the control factories (a button IS a BoxEl, so the role is how it announces itself). Carried on the input/a11y column; surfaced to the future UIA layer (ControlType), devtools, and UI tests. Default = a plain box/container.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.BlurHash
files.Summary: BlurHash decoder — turns a compact ~20–30-char string into a tiny blurred preview (the LQIP placeholder used by Next.js blurDataURL, SwiftUI/Nuke BlurHash). The engine uploads the decoded tile as an image's initial texture so a recognizable blur shows instantly while the full-res art decodes off-thread. Pure math (no deps), AOT-safe. Algorithm: github.com/woltapp/blurhash.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.BrushHandle
files.Summary: Typed wrapper for brushes interned in the BrushTable.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.CharEventArgs
files.Summary: Character (text) input passed to OnCharInput handlers — the layout/IME-resolved Unicode codepoint, distinct from the raw virtual-key of (Win32 splits WM_KEYDOWN from WM_CHAR; we mirror that).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.ColorF
files.Summary: Straight-alpha sRGB color (P8: brush color = straight-alpha sRGB float4).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.Diag
files.Summary:
The standardized, reusable engine-wide diagnostics facility. Every subsystem instruments through it the same way: Diag.Count("text.glyph","rasterized"), Diag.Set("d3d12","glyphInstances", n), Diag.Event("rhi","device-lost"), using (Diag.Time("frame","record")) { … }.

COST MODEL: the recording methods are [Conditional("DEBUG"), Conditional("FLUENTGPU_DIAG")], so on a Release build (with neither symbol defined) the compiler removes the call site AND the argument evaluation entirely — a Diag.Set("text.atlas","nonZero", ExpensiveScan()) costs literally nothing in production. Define FLUENTGPU_DIAG to keep diagnostics in a Release build; toggle at runtime with the AppContext switch "FluentGpu.Diagnostics" or by setting . Route output by setting .

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.DiagScope
files.Summary: Timing scope from . Zero-work on Release (the Diag.CompiledIn const folds out).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.DragEventArgs
files.Summary: Drag-reorder lifecycle payload for OnDragStarted/OnDragDelta/OnDragCompleted: the pointer in the dragged node's CURRENT box ( stays ≈ the grab offset) and in window space, the accumulated gesture translation since the arming press, and the smoothed pointer velocity (px/s, ~50ms EMA) for flick/settle decisions. ONE instance is reused for the whole gesture (0 steady-state alloc per move) — handlers must copy fields they keep, never hold the reference.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.DragSession
files.Summary: THE live drag session (E5-L2): ONE mutable instance owned by Input.DragDropContext, opened when an L1 drag promotes on a chain carrying a (payload resolved once), updated per pointer move, handed to every handler, and cleared (incl. the Payload GC edge) when the gesture ends. Handlers copy what they keep — never hold the reference across gestures.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.DragSource
files.Summary: E5-L2 typed drag SOURCE spec (BoxEl.Draggable — the Flutter Draggable / react-beautiful-dnd model; user ruling 2026-06-10: deliberately NOT WinUI's OLE DataPackage/DoDragDrop modal loop): Kind is a string discriminator so target accept-tests are cast-free; PayloadFactory resolves the typed payload ONCE when the L1 press promotes past the drag box (never per move). Trimming-safe: plain delegates.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.DropEffect
files.Summary: The semantic outcome of an IN-APP drop (E5-L2 — the Flutter/SwiftUI model, never OLE): advisory — the engine sets Move while over an accepting target, None otherwise; targets may refine it in OnEnter/OnOver.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.DropTargetSpec
files.Summary: E5-L2 drop TARGET spec (BoxEl.DropTarget — Flutter DragTarget / SwiftUI dropDestination): receives sessions whose Kind is in . Discovery is hit-test-chain based — per pointer move the engine picks the NEAREST enabled accepting target under the pointer (a non-accepting target never blocks an accepting ancestor). OnEnter/OnLeave fire on hover transitions, OnOver every move while inside, OnDrop on release over it (BEFORE the L1 completion). The argument is THE one live reused instance — copy what you keep.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.DynamicTextKind
files.Summary: Retained text slots whose contents are refreshed by the host without re-rendering or relayout.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.DynamicsKind
files.Summary: Spring (velocity-carrying, interruptible — the default) or an eased fixed-duration tween.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.Easing
files.Summary: Easing curves — a FOUNDATIONAL motion primitive shared by every layer that animates: the animation-track engine, declarative transitions, and the image placeholder→art cross-fade. Living in Foundation lets any subsystem (Dsl, Scene, Render, Animation) spec a transition with the same vocabulary. Includes the real Fluent 2 / WinUI curves.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.Easings
files.Summary: Evaluates an curve at normalized time t (0..1).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.Edges4
files.Summary: Per-edge thickness (margin/padding/border). [InlineArray]-shaped logically; struct of 4 floats here.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.EnterExit
files.Summary: Presented-space terminal for an inserted/removed node: where it animates FROM on enter / TO on exit (offset + scale + opacity), relative to its laid-out rect. Active=false ⇒ the node simply snaps in/out.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.FlexAlign
files.Summary: Cross-axis alignment (flexbox align-items / align-self). on a child = inherit the container's align-items.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.FlexJustify
files.Summary: Main-axis distribution of free space (flexbox justify-content).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.GradientSpec
files.Summary: A gradient fill. is the linear axis (0 = left-to-right, 90 = top-to-bottom). Up to 4 stops are carried into the POD draw command; extras are ignored. When set on a node it supersedes the solid fill at record time.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.GradientStop
files.Summary: One gradient stop: a normalized position (0..1 along the gradient axis) and its color.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.Handle
files.Summary: Generational handle — 8 bytes {u32 index, u32 gen}. The canonical FluentGpu handle layout.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.ImageFit
files.Summary: How an image's pixels map into its (possibly differently-shaped) layout box — the CSS object-fit model, cleaner than WinUI's Stretch because box-sizing is a separate concern (see AspectRatio). Default (what album art / thumbnails want): never distorts, never letterboxes.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.ImageTransition
files.Summary: The placeholder→image reveal transition (the cross-fade) — spec'd with the engine's shared motion vocabulary (a duration in ms + an curve) so image reveals animate exactly like every other control. Override per-image via ImageEl.Transition, or change the app-wide once at startup. Set = 0 (or use ) to DISABLE the fade — the image then appears instantly.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.KeyAccelerator
files.Summary: A keyboard accelerator chord (WinUI KeyboardAccelerator): + invoke the owning node's click handler from anywhere (dispatched after focused routing leaves the key unhandled).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.KeyEventArgs
files.Summary: Keyboard event passed to node handlers during tunnel/bubble routing. stops propagation. Carries the modifier chord and the auto-repeat flag (Win32 lParam bit 30) so editors can do Ctrl+arrow word navigation and Shift+arrow selection without re-querying the keyboard.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.KeyModifiers
files.Summary: Keyboard modifier state, captured per input event at the platform pump (Win32 GetKeyState).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.Keys
files.Summary: Well-known virtual-key codes (Win32 VK_*) used by the input router.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.LayoutTransition
files.Summary: The whole authoring surface (interned POD). Channels × dynamics × size-mode × enter/exit compose orthogonally — translate / scale / rotate / opacity / clip-reveal all fall out, with no per-control special cases.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.LineStacking
files.Summary: How an explicit LineHeight interacts with the font-natural line box (mirrors WinUI LineStackingStrategy; BaselineToBaseline is not modeled). MaxHeight — the WinUI BaseTextBlockStyle default (TextBlock_themeresources.xaml:16) — makes the line advance max(font-natural, LineHeight); BlockLineHeight makes the line advance exactly LineHeight (lines may visually overlap when smaller than natural).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.NativeHandle
files.Summary: POD across the PAL/RHI seam — never a boxed object. The D3D12 leaf switches on .

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.NodeFlags
files.Summary: Per-node dirty + state bits (a single 32-bit SceneStore column). Three orthogonal dirty axes.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.NodeHandle
files.Summary: Zero-cost typed wrapper over for nodes in the SceneStore.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.ObjectPool<T>
files.Summary: Edge-only recycle pool for managed objects (Component / RenderContext). Cap-bounded.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.PngWriter
files.Summary: Minimal pure-managed PNG encoder for debug frame captures — no WIC / System.Drawing, AOT-safe, zero new deps. Emits an opaque RGB PNG (color type 2) from a tightly-packed top-down BGRA8 buffer (the D3D12 readback format). Used only by the --screenshot tooling path; never on the hot frame path.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.PointerEventArgs
files.Summary: Position-aware pointer-press payload for OnPointerPressed: local coords + the click count (1/2/3 — the dispatcher tracks double/triple-click timing and slop), the modifier chord, the button (0=left 1=right 2=middle) and the device kind. Allocated only on an actual press (cold user-gesture edge).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.PointerKind
files.Summary: The physical pointer device class an event came from (WinUI PointerDeviceType) — controls touch-vs-mouse behavioral splits (RatingControl hover scale, touch paddings).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.PolylineStrokeSpec
files.Summary: A retained stroked polyline with normalized trim controls. Points are local DIP coordinates inside the node's box; trim values are path-distance fractions (0..1), so animation can reveal the stroke in draw order.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.RectF
files.Summary: Axis-aligned rectangle in DIPs. Local to its node (P8: Bounds are node-LOCAL).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.ShadowSpec
files.Summary: A drop shadow / elevation descriptor (CSS box-shadow shape). Lives in Foundation so Scene/Render/Reconciler can read it without referencing Dsl. Carried by an elevated node; the recorder emits a DrawShadow beneath the fill.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.SizeAnchor
files.Summary: Which edge of a node its CONTENT is anchored to while the size animates. = content stays put, the far edge sweeps (a wipe). = the content's end edge rides the animated edge (the WinUI Expander "slide out from under the header": at reveal 0 the content sits fully behind the leading edge; its trailing rounded corners stay visible mid-motion). Applied by the recorder as a child-group offset — compositor-composed, no per-child knowledge.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.SizeMode
files.Summary: How a SIZE change is animated. picks per node: pure-chrome leaf → ; a text/wrapping subtree under budget → ; everything else → .

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.SlabAllocator<T>
files.Summary: Stable, generation-versioned slab over T. Index 0 is reserved as the null slot, so default(Handle) is null. Generation is bumped on Free (ABA defense). O(1) alloc/free via an intrusive int free-list. Pointer-bearing T uses a side _next array (we always do, here).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.SpanRect
files.Summary: One laid-out rect artifact of a span run (paragraph-node-local DIP): the line-fragment geometry of a decorated/link span. is the single flag bit it serves — Link rects are the FULL line-fragment band (hit-testing, Hand cursor); Underline/Strikethrough rects are the positioned BARS (the recorder fills them verbatim, no font-seam touch at record time).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.SpanRun
files.Summary: A registered span run: the immutable per-span shaping overlays plus the seam-published layout artifacts. Lives in ; referenced from TextStyle.SpanRunId (the measure-cache / shaped-run-cache key), so a style change ⇒ a fresh run id ⇒ every downstream cache self-invalidates.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.SpanRunRects
files.Summary: An immutable snapshot of a span run's laid-out rect artifacts at one wrap width. Published whole by the text seam on a measure-cache miss (content/width change) and read lock-free by the recorder (decoration bars) and the input dispatcher (hyperlink cursor/click) — steady frames never re-touch the font seam.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.SpanRunTable
files.Summary:
Process-wide registry of span runs — the interner-style side channel that lets the multi-run style overlay ride an int through the POD pipeline (TextStyle.SpanRunId → DrawList DrawGlyphRunCmd.SpanRunId) the way strings ride through .

Seam-safe (threading-render-seam.md §9, the StringTable discipline): chunked backing store — chunks are allocated once and never moved; a release-store of the count publishes each new slot, the reader acquire-loads the count before indexing. The UI thread is the single writer (reconciler, at element-change time — never in frame phases 6–13); the render-side glyph path resolves the previous frame's ids concurrently.

Static (deliberate): unlike (host-instanced and threaded through every constructor), the render-side consumer reaches a span run only through the POD id baked in the draw stream — there is no host seam to hand an instance through. One process = one UI thread = one writer, ids burn monotonically and are never reused, so a process-global table is safe by the same argument that makes the ids safe.

Reclamation: owners (the scene's span-text side-table) pair /; a run whose count hits 0 has its slot cleared after a creation-distance quarantine (≥ newer runs registered — far beyond the in-flight frame depth at any realistic churn), so a draw list recorded just before the release still resolves. A cleared id resolves null → consumers fall back to the base style. Ids are never reused.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.SpanStyle
files.Summary: The POD shaping overlay for one span of a span run: the UTF-16 char range [, ) of the CONCATENATED paragraph text plus the style deltas the text seam applies over the base TextStyle. Weight 0 / SizeDip 0 / FontFamily empty / Color A==0 = inherit the base. Built by the reconciler from s; resolved by both text backends and the glyph renderer through , so measure ≡ hit-test ≡ render for span runs too.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.StringId
files.Summary: 4-byte interned string id. Id 0 = empty. No reaches the paint path.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.StringTable
files.Summary:
String interner with REF-COUNTED reclamation. Lives in Foundation so Render can resolve without depending on Scene.

 <p><b>Seam-safe (threading-render-seam.md §9):</b> the UI thread interns (<xref href="FluentGpu.Foundation.StringTable.Intern(System.String)" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref>, the single writer) while
 the render thread resolves (<xref href="FluentGpu.Foundation.StringTable.Resolve(FluentGpu.Foundation.StringId)" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref>) the previous frame's ids concurrently. The backing store is therefore
 <i>chunked</i>: chunks are allocated once and never resized or moved, so a concurrent reader can never observe a torn or
 reallocated array (a plain <code>List<string></code> would race on its geometric Add-realloc). A release-store of the count
 publishes each new slot; the reader acquire-loads the count before indexing, so it only ever reads fully-written slots.</p>

 <p><b>Reclamation (virtualization workloads):</b> a 100k-row virtual list streams unique row text through the table —
 append-only interning is an unbounded leak. Owners (the scene's text columns) pair <xref href="FluentGpu.Foundation.StringTable.AddRef(FluentGpu.Foundation.StringId)" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref>/<xref href="FluentGpu.Foundation.StringTable.Release(FluentGpu.Foundation.StringId)" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref>;
 when an id's count hits 0 its map entry is removed (a re-intern of the same content gets a NEW id) and the slot is cleared
