 after a reader quarantine (<xref href="FluentGpu.Foundation.StringTable.Tick" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref> per frame, far beyond the in-flight frame depth). <b>Ids are never reused</b> —
 reclaimed ids stay burned, so a stale id can never alias different content (the shaped-run cache keys on ids and ages out in
 well under the id space). Fully-dead chunks free their backing array. Strings never AddRef'd are simply permanent.</p>

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.TextLineBounds
files.Summary: Which vertical band a text line reports as its box (mirrors WinUI TextLineBounds, minimum set — TrimToCapHeight/TrimToBaseline individually are not modeled). Full — the WinUI BaseTextBlockStyle default (TextBlock_themeresources.xaml:17) — is the font-natural ascent..descent box; Tight trims the box to cap-height..baseline so vertical centering is optical (e.g. PersonPicture initials, PersonPicture.xaml:66).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.TextSpan
files.Summary:
One inline run of a rich-text paragraph (the WinUI TextBlock.Inlines Run/Bold/Hyperlink model, rtb-01). A SpanTextEl paragraph is an array of these; the WHOLE paragraph shapes as ONE flow (spans concatenated, wrapped together — exactly how WinUI lays a paragraph's inline collection, RichTextBlock.g.h:63 get_Blocks).

0 / null / null / A==0 all mean "inherit the paragraph base". non-null makes this a HYPERLINK span: the engine resolves the Hand cursor over the span's laid-out rects and fires the action on click/tap — WinUI's inline Hyperlink (SetCursor(MouseCursorHand) on hyperlink hover, RichTextBlock.cpp:2995 / TextBlock.cpp:3488). Accent foreground + underline styling is the CALLER's job (HyperlinkForeground theming, generic.xaml:1120-1122).

No italic axis: the shaper resolves faces by (family, weight) only — DWRITE_FONT_STYLE_NORMAL is fixed (TextLayoutEngine face resolution); FontStyle arrives with the italics workstream (plan §Text display).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.TextTrim
files.Summary: How overflowing text is truncated (mirrors WinUI TextTrimming). Applies to the last visible line.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.TextWrap
files.Summary: How a text run breaks across lines (mirrors WinUI TextWrapping).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.TrackKind
files.Summary: A CSS-Grid track sizing function. (Lives in Foundation so both the DSL and the layout/scene see it.)

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.TransitionChannels
files.Summary: The general layout-transition spec — the entire authoring surface for "animate this node's layout changes". A plain POD (no closures) so it is hot-path safe, and it lives in Foundation (below the AnimEngine, which reads it and converts the dynamics to its internal spring/tween). It attaches to a node via BoxEl.Animate (or RenderContext.UseLayoutAnimation); the host diffs the node's presented rect vs its new laid-out rect each commit and drives the presented geometry toward target through these channels/dynamics — no relayout, no per-frame re-render, and zero control-type knowledge.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.TransitionDynamics
files.Summary: The dynamics of a transition. Springs are the default (interruptible, jump-free retarget): Response ≈ the settle time in seconds, DampingRatio 1 = critical (no overshoot), <1 = bouncy. A tween is opt-in: fixed DurationMs + Easing (an , so authored WinUI KeySplines are expressible; a default spec is normalized by the engine to ). Stored as scalars (not the AnimEngine's SpringParams) so the spec can live in Foundation; the engine converts at seed time. A default value (all zero) is normalized by the engine to the spring defaults.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Foundation.WheelEventArgs
files.Summary: Element-level wheel payload for OnPointerWheel (WinUI PointerWheelChanged): the platform wheel delta — the same value the viewport scroll path consumes — plus the modifier chord. Setting stops the dispatcher from scrolling the enclosing viewport (NumberBox value-stepping inside a scrollable form, NumberBox.cpp:578-597 OnNumberBoxScroll marks the routed event handled). Unhandled, the wheel keeps walking up to the next handler / the nearest scrollable (routed-event semantics). Allocated per wheel event (cold edge).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Hooks.Component
files.Summary: Base for stateful components. Override ; use hooks in stable order each render.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Hooks.ComponentEl
files.Summary: An element that embeds a child (with its own hooks/state) into a parent's render output. The reconciler instantiates the component once (via ), reuses it across renders of the same , and reconciles its rendered output as this node's content. This is what lets the framework compose many stateful components rather than one root.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Hooks.Context<T>
files.Summary: A context channel with a default. Consumers read the nearest provider's value via UseContext.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Hooks.ContextProviderEl
files.Summary: Provides a context value to its subtree (the React Context.Provider). One child.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Hooks.ContextStack
files.Summary: Reconcile-time shadow stack of provided context values. The reconciler pushes a provider's value before reconciling its subtree (incl. nested component renders) and pops after; UseContext reads the top. (Push/pop happen on the dirty reconcile path, never the zero-alloc paint half.)

files.Title: FluentGpu.Hooks.Ctx
files.Summary: Fluent: Ctx.Provide(MyContext, value, child).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Hooks.Embed
files.Summary: Fluent helper: Embed.Comp(() => new MyComponent()) embeds a stateful child component.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Hooks.Flow
files.Summary: Fluent reactive control-flow: Flow.Show(() => open.Value, panel), Flow.For(() => xs.Value.Count, i => Row(xs.Value[i])).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Hooks.ForEl
files.Summary: Reactive keyed list (the Solid <For>). / read signals; the reconciler runs them inside a boundary effect and re-realizes the children through the existing keyed diff when the list changes — rows are created/moved/removed (and their state preserved by key), with no parent re-render.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Hooks.FrameClock
files.Summary: A per-frame tick the host bumps (only while something subscribes, so it's free when idle). A tree-level concern that must poll each frame — e.g. an overlay driving a timed close animation to completion — reads UseContext(FrameClock.Tick) to re-render every frame for as long as it's mounted.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Hooks.InputHooks
files.Summary: A host-owned registration surface a tree-level concern can hook into without the host depending on it. The host bridges into the input dispatcher's pre-focus key hook, and runs AfterAnimations after the animation engine ticks but before recording. Read the host instance via UseContext(InputHooks.Current).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Hooks.MotionHooks
files.Summary: Reusable Fluent motion patterns built on the component's animation hooks. The entrance mirrors WinUI's implicit show transition (Offset From 0,24 + Opacity 0→1, 400/200ms, the real Fluent decelerate curve). Honors .

files.Title: FluentGpu.Hooks.ReactiveComponent
files.Summary: A signals-native component: runs ONCE at mount (the SolidJS model) — create signals, wire bindings (TransformBind/For/Show) and return the tree. The component never re-renders; fine-grained bindings update exactly the nodes that read a changed signal. Use this for the highest-performance UI; use plain when you prefer the re-render model (still granular).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Hooks.Ref<T>
files.Summary: A stable, mutable per-component box that persists across renders (the React useRef container).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Hooks.RenderContext
files.Summary: Per-component hook storage (ordered; rules-of-hooks = stable call order). In the signals-first model a component is a reactive computation (its render-effect); is a whose only subscriber is that render-effect, so a setState re-renders ONLY this component's subtree (granular), and subscribes the render-effect to the provider's signal so a context change propagates to exactly the consumers that read it. High-frequency scalars get bound straight to a node channel — no re-render at all.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Hooks.ShowEl
files.Summary: Reactive conditional (the Solid <Show>). reads signals; the reconciler runs it inside a boundary effect and mounts / as the condition flips — structural change only, no parent re-render.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Hooks.Viewport
files.Summary: The ambient client size (DIP), pushed by the host each frame so components can adapt to available width (responsive layout / NavigationView display modes). Read with UseContext(Viewport.Size).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Hosting.PopupWindowSlot
files.Summary: Composition root + the single-UI-thread frame loop. Signals-first: a setState writes a signal that schedules ONLY the owning component's render-effect (granular), and a bound high-frequency scalar (slider/scroll) writes a node channel directly — a compositor-only frame with no render/reconcile/layout. The host drains the reactive runtime once per frame (phase 3), runs (scoped) layout only when a reconcile/layout-bind changed something, then records.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Input.DragController
files.Summary:
The drag-reorder gesture engine (E5). A left press on (or inside) a CanDrag node ARMS a candidate; pointer travel outside the drag box PROMOTES it from a click to a drag (the dispatcher then suppresses the click and routes every move here — capture semantics). The promoted drag draws its visual on the dragged node itself: a parent-space LocalTransform translate + 0.8 opacity + a flyout-class shadow (WinUI ListViewItemDragThemeOpacity = 0.80 — microsoft-ui-xaml controls\dev\CommonStyles\ListViewItem_themeresources.xaml:7), and the node stops hit-testing so drop-target queries see THROUGH the moving visual.

Threshold: the Windows drag box is SM_CXDRAG/SM_CYDRAG (4px default), tested per-axis (dx > maxDx || dy > maxDy — microsoft-ui-xaml dxaml\xcp\dxaml\lib\ListViewBaseItem_Partial.cpp:1864-1878). WinUI's list items double it (LISTVIEWBASEITEM_MOUSE_DRAG_THRESHOLD_MULTIPLIER = 2.0, same file :54); this engine uses the 4px base box per plan E5, matching .

Axis-aware arena-lite (promotion-time arbitration): the item's reorder axis is its PARENT container's main axis (row → horizontal item-drag). A gesture whose dominant axis is PERPENDICULAR to that, with a scrollable ancestor that actually overflows along the gesture axis, yields to the pan (vertical pan over a scrollable beats a horizontal item-drag) — the WinUI manipulation-arena outcome for a tab strip inside a scrolling page.

Drop-settle rides the existing FLIP pipeline: Complete restores the resting visuals and reports (draggedRect → restingRect) through ; the host wires that to AnimEngine.AnimateBounds, so the seeded position spring is retargeted velocity-continuously by the same commit's ApplyProjections when the app's OnDragCompleted handler reorders the collection — displaced siblings and the dropped item all animate through the one layout-transition path (reorder hints come free).

Mid-drag live reorder rides the same pipeline: when the consumer re-projects the children ORDER at the dwell-committed target (ReorderList.ProjectOrder), displaced siblings FLIP to their new slots while RE-ANCHORS the pointer-held visual — it strips the translate it last applied to find the node's CURRENT resting origin, then aims at (grab origin + gesture delta), so a slot move under the pointer (or an ancestor scrolling) never jumps the visual (the WinUI outcome: the dragged item tracks the pointer while MoveItemsForLiveReorder shifts the rest — ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp:2254). The drag visuals are re-asserted on every move because a mid-drag commit's patch restores the authored opacity/shadow/hit-test (Reconciler ApplyBox writes them unconditionally).

GHOST (E5-L2): while a drag is active the lifted node carries and is published as — the recorder excludes it from the clipped main pass and re-walks its subtree in an UNCLIPPED top band emitted last (mirroring the orphan pass), so the visual escapes every ancestor scissor (a row dragged out of a clipped list keeps drawing) and paints above overlays — the Flutter/rbd ghost layer.

Spring-lag follow (the rbd feel): the PRESENTED translate eases toward the gesture target with a critically-damped spring () instead of pinning rigidly — 0-alloc per move/tick. It engages only when the platform delivers real timestamps (consecutive non-zero TimestampMs); 0-stamp gestures (the headless default) snap presented == target, keeping every position assertion deterministic. false disables it outright. The host advances in-between-move easing (and the pointer-pin while an edge auto-scroll moves the resting origin under a still pointer) via .

files.Title: FluentGpu.Input.DragDropContext
files.Summary:
L2 of the drag-drop stack (plan E5; user ruling 2026-06-10: deliberately NOT WinUI's OLE DataPackage/DoDragDrop modal loop — the Flutter Draggable/DragTarget + react-beautiful-dnd context + SwiftUI draggable/dropDestination model instead; the frame loop STAYS LIVE for the whole gesture). Layered on the L1 gesture:

• A BoxEl.Draggable (: a string Kind discriminator + a payload factory) marks a node a typed drag source. When the L1 press promotes past the drag box, the dispatcher calls — the nearest enabled DragSource up the chain opens THE session and its resolves ONCE (cold gesture edge; never per-move). • A BoxEl.DropTarget () makes ANY surface a candidate receiver. Per pointer move the dispatcher hands the hit-test chain under the pointer; the nearest enabled target whose AcceptKinds contains the session's Kind becomes (a non-accepting target never blocks an accepting ancestor — the Flutter DragTarget pass-through). The dragged subtree can never self-target: L1 already cleared its HitTestVisible, so hit-testing sees THROUGH the moving visual. • Enter/Leave fire on target transitions, Over on every move while inside, Drop on release over an accepting target ( — fired BEFORE the L1 completion so the target reads the live session). The ONE mutable instance is reused for the whole gesture (0 steady-state alloc per move); handlers copy what they keep.

Edge auto-scroll (the rbd/WinUI "drag near the viewport edge keeps scrolling" behavior) is engine-level here so every drag gets it: while a session is live, the pointer entering the hot zone of the nearest OVERFLOWING scrollable ancestor arms a proportional scroll velocity the host drives via . Constants and behavior are WinUI's (microsoft-ui-xaml dxaml\xcp\dxaml\lib\ListViewBase_Partial_Reorder.cpp): a 100px edge band (:39 LISTVIEWBASE_EDGE_SCROLL_EDGE_WIDTH_PX), speed varying LINEARLY from 1500 px/s at the edge to 150 px/s at the band's inner rim (:42-47), a 50ms delay-start with instant velocity updates once running (:40, :1749-1753), the start-edge tried before the end-edge (:1660-1707), and scrolling suppressed when already pinned to that boundary (:1686-1690, :1718-1722).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Input.FocusDirection
files.Summary: Directional focus movement for roving/XY keyboard navigation (arrow keys in lists, grids, menus).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Input.InputDispatcher
files.Summary: Phase 2 (input dispatch): hit-tests the committed scene and routes pointer + keyboard. Pointer down→up over the same node fires the click handler and focuses the nearest focusable self-or-ancestor (a WinUI IsTabStop=False part never takes pointer focus; nothing focusable in the chain → focus unchanged); keyboard routes to the focused node and bubbles up ancestors (Handled stops it); Tab moves focus through focusable nodes; Enter/Space activates a focused clickable (the "one declaration, three modalities" contract). The full engine adds tunnel(Preview), gesture arena, XY-focus.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Layout.FlexLayout
files.Summary: Flexbox layout over the SoA scene columns. Two descents: Measure (bottom-up — content/basis base sizes, honoring explicit size + min/max + text content) then Arrange (top-down — distribute free space by flex-grow/shrink, position by justify-content, align the cross axis by align-items/align-self incl. stretch, applying margins). Direction 0 = row (main = X), 1 = column (main = Y). Wrap / grid / absolute positioning are the remaining layout work.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Layout.LayoutInvalidator
files.Summary: Scoped relayout (layout.md §4): consumes the SceneStore's LayoutDirty worklist and, for each dirty node, walks UP to the nearest layout boundary — a fixed-size, non-flexing, clipped container whose own size cannot change due to a descendant — then re-solves just that subtree (). The boundary is the firewall: a setState deep inside a fixed-size card relayouts only the card, never the page. Falls back to a full layout from the root when a dirty node has no bounded ancestor.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Media.Codecs.Wic.WicImageCodec
files.Summary: Windows leaf (media-pipeline.md §3): Windows Imaging Component constrained decode. Decodes straight to the target bucket size via IWICBitmapScaler (a 3000px cover never materializes full-res in CPU memory) and converts to 32bppPBGRA (premultiplied BGRA — matches the engine's blend posture and the GPU texture format). All WIC COM objects are created and used ENTIRELY on the calling worker thread (per-thread cached factory) and never cross the scheduler seam — only POD pixels do (the FGCOM per-thread-COM rule).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Media.DecodeOptions
files.Summary: Decode/fetch robustness policy. Defaults tuned for album art over flaky links (slow internet, transient 5xx).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Media.DecodeScheduler
files.Summary: Off-thread, parallel, non-blocking, PRIORITIZED image decoder (media-pipeline.md §3). Implements the portable seam the drives: enqueues into one of three priority lanes (Visible > Overscan > Prefetch) — a non-blocking write on the UI thread; a pool of N worker tasks drain the highest non-empty lane and fetch+decode CONCURRENTLY; and drains finished results on the UI thread and never waits on a decode. promotes a queued prefetch that just scrolled into view; drops a queued/in-flight decode whose row recycled. Robustness: per-attempt timeout, transient retry with exponential backoff, permanent fail-fast (see ). Under backpressure the lowest off-screen lane is dropped — never Visible. Diagnostics post to the media counter group.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Media.DefaultImageFetcher
files.Summary:
HTTP(S) + local-file fetcher, configured per the cross-ecosystem consensus (Flutter cache_manager, iOS URLSession, OkHttp/Coil, browsers):

  • ONE pooled — never new HttpClient() per request (socket exhaustion).
  • recycles connections so DNS is re-resolved (stale-DNS / CDN-edge rotation fix) without IHttpClientFactory.
  • HTTP/2 with multiple connections — CDN request multiplexing; bounded MaxConnectionsPerServer.
  • Automatic decompression; per-request deadline via the token (NOT the global HttpClient.Timeout).
  • Disk-first: a persistent serves instant, offline, restart-surviving hits.
  • Streams the body into an ArrayPool buffer — no per-fetch byte[].

Reuse ONE instance app-wide. Maps transport/HTTP-status to for transient-vs-permanent.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Media.DiskImageCache
files.Summary: Persistent on-disk cache of ENCODED image bytes (the second tier under the in-memory GPU residency cache) — the memory+disk model every mature loader uses (Flutter flutter_cache_manager, Nuke/Kingfisher DataCache, SDWebImage). Content-addressed by a hash of the source URL, with an LRU byte budget + max-object count + stale period. It makes CDN images instant on the second view, survives app restarts, and serves offline. Stores the compressed source (≈tens of KB), NOT the decoded BGRA (which is the memory tier) — so the budget covers thousands of covers.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Media.FetchResult
files.Summary: The outcome of a fetch attempt. On success it hands back a buffer RENTED FROM plus the valid length — the scheduler returns it to the pool after decode, so a fetch costs no net managed allocation. On failure it carries a classified the scheduler uses to pick transient-retry vs permanent-fail.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Media.IImageCodec
files.Summary: Portable decode seam (media-pipeline.md §3): turn encoded bytes (JPEG/PNG/WebP/…) into PREMULTIPLIED BGRA8 at a target size, constrained so a huge source never materializes full-res in CPU memory (WIC IWICBitmapScaler on Windows, CGImageSource on macOS). Implemented entirely on a worker thread; the leaf's COM/objc objects never cross the scheduler seam — only POD pixels do.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Media.IImageFetcher
files.Summary: Portable fetch seam (media-pipeline.md §3 "Fetch"): turn a source URI into encoded bytes off the UI thread. Maps transport/HTTP-status errors to so the scheduler can distinguish transient (network, timeout, 5xx → retried) from permanent (404, other 4xx → not retried). Honors the cancellation token (per-attempt timeout + shutdown). HTTP(S) + local file by default ().

files.Title: FluentGpu.Pal.IClipboard
files.Summary: The system-clipboard seam (plain Unicode text, the TextBox cut/copy/paste contract). UI-thread only. is the OS clipboard epoch (Win32 GetClipboardSequenceNumber) — context menus gate their Paste item on it changing instead of polling content (the ISystemColors versioned-external-store idiom).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Pal.IPlatformLocale
files.Summary: Versioned external-store-shaped locale seam (modeled on ISystemColors). L9.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Pal.IPlatformPopupWindow
files.Summary: A borderless, non-activating top-level popup surface (the PAL seam for WinUI windowed popups / E4 out-of-bounds flyouts; later the substrate for E10 tear-out windows). The host owns rendering into it (its own swapchain on ); the popup never owns engine state. All bounds are physical virtual-screen px.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Pal.IPlatformTextInput
files.Summary: The per-window IME/text-services seam (Imm32 today; the interface is event-shaped so a TSF/ITextStoreACP implementation can replace it without touching the engine — full in-place TSF is a named hardening item).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Pal.ITextInputSink
files.Summary: Receives IME composition events from the platform (the focused editor registers as the sink through the input router). Composition TEXT cannot ride the POD (strings) — the platform invokes the sink directly on the UI thread during the message pump; the typing edge is allowed bounded Gen0.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Pal.ImeClause
files.Summary: One clause of the in-flight composition string (UTF-16 char range, local to the composition).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Pal.ImeClauseKind
files.Summary: How an IME composition clause renders (Imm32 ATTR_* / TSF display attributes): Input = dotted underline (not yet converted), Converted = solid underline, TargetConverted/TargetNotConverted = the clause the candidate window currently targets (thick underline).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Pal.InputEvent
files.Summary: POD input event drained from the host-owned ring once per frame (no C# events across the seam). ScrollDelta (Wheel only) is in DIP, oriented so positive = scroll toward the content end (offset increases). The platform pump converts WM_MOUSEWHEEL notches → DIP and flips the sign there. Button: 0 = left, 1 = right, 2 = middle. Mods is the modifier chord at the time of the event (pump-captured); IsRepeat = keyboard auto-repeat (lParam bit 30); TimestampMs = the platform message time (drives double/triple-click detection in the dispatcher).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Pal.InputEventRing
files.Summary: Drained by the host each frame; the window writes POD events into it (move-coalesced).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Pal.PopupWindowDesc
files.Summary: Creation parameters for a popup window. Owner = the owning top-level window (the popup stays above it in z-order and never takes activation); BoundsPx = initial bounds in physical virtual-screen px (may be empty — set real bounds via before Show).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Pal.SystemParams
files.Summary: OS user-preference parameters the engine honors (WinUI reads them through SystemParametersInfo / the registry). The platform writes them ONCE at startup (Win32: HKCU + SPI reads); headless keeps the defaults for determinism. Read anywhere (controls included) — plain statics, no per-frame cost.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Pal.TitleBarHit
files.Summary: Non-client classification for one engine-reported titlebar rect (engine → WM_NCHITTEST). Client marks an INTERACTIVE ISLAND (search box, back/pane buttons) the engine keeps; Caption is the OS drag-move band; the three buttons get HTMIN/HTMAX/HTCLOSE so Win11 shows the snap-layouts flyout over Max.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Pal.TitleBarRegion
files.Summary: One reported titlebar region: a rect in CLIENT DIP (the engine's space) + its non-client classification. Pushed on titlebar relayout only (push-on-change — never per frame). First match wins at hit-test, so callers list interactive islands and buttons BEFORE the catch-all band.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Pal.WindowDesc
files.Summary: Composited = the window is composited with per-pixel alpha (WS_EX_NOREDIRECTIONBITMAP) so a DirectComposition swapchain can show the DWM Mica backdrop through transparent pixels. CustomFrame = the engine draws the ENTIRE titlebar (WinUI ExtendsContentIntoTitleBar): the platform strips the OS caption (WM_NCCALCSIZE) but keeps the resize frame/shadow, answers WM_NCHITTEST from the engine-reported s, and synthesizes pointer input for the engine-drawn caption buttons.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Pal.WindowState
files.Summary: Window placement, surfaced for custom-titlebar chrome ().

files.Title: FluentGpu.Pal.Windows.Win32App
files.Summary: Real Win32 PAL: CreateWindowExW + a static WndProc + a PeekMessage pump that drains WM_* into POD input.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Pal.Windows.Win32Clipboard
files.Summary: Win32 clipboard (CF_UNICODETEXT). UI-thread only; opens are retried briefly (another app may hold it).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Pal.Windows.Win32PopupWindow
files.Summary:
A borderless, non-activating, owned top-level popup HWND — the Win32 backing of and the engine analogue of WinUI's windowed CPopup (microsoft-ui-xaml Popup_Partial.cpp:1019 SetIsWindowed() creates an HWND via PopupSiteBridge so flyouts/menus can render OUTSIDE the XAML window; Popup_Partial.cpp:951-970 IsConstrainedToRootBounds == false for windowed popups).

  • WS_POPUP + owner: borderless, z-ordered above the owner, hidden when the owner minimizes.
  • WS_EX_NOACTIVATE + MA_NOACTIVATE: the popup NEVER takes activation — keyboard focus stays on the owner window exactly like WinUI's popup site (the dispatcher keeps routing keys to the main window).
  • WS_EX_TOOLWINDOW: no taskbar button / Alt-Tab entry.
  • WS_EX_NOREDIRECTIONBITMAP: no GDI redirection surface — the host presents into it with its own composition swapchain (same composited-surface model as the main window).
  • Mouse input over the popup is FORWARDED to the owner , translated popup-client → screen → owner-client → owner DIP, so the one InputDispatcher hit-tests the popup subtree at its scene coordinates (the subtree stays in the single SceneStore; out-of-bounds coords hit-test fine).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Pal.Windows.Win32TextInput
files.Summary: Imm32 IME plumbing for one window: suppresses the system composition window, reads the composition string + caret + clause attributes on WM_IME_COMPOSITION, forwards them to the registered sink, and positions the candidate window at the caret (CFS_EXCLUDE). Reused buffers — the typing edge allocates only event strings.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Pal.Windows.Win32Theme
files.Summary: Windows system-theme integration: the real accent color + DWM dark titlebar / Mica backdrop.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Reconciler.TreeReconciler
files.Summary: Patches the retained SceneStore from an immutable Element tree. Signals-first: every component is a reactive render-effect (re-renders + reconciles ONLY its own subtree when its state/context changes — granular, never the whole app); fine-grained bindings (TransformBind/OpacityBind/…) and reactive control-flow (/ ) are effects too. The keyed positional+type diff is retained as the STRUCTURAL engine (used on re-render and behind For/Show); a reused component on a parent re-render is a no-op (it is autonomous).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Reconciler.VirtualListEl
files.Summary:
A virtualized collection viewport: ONE retained node + a window of keyed children. Each frame the visible range is dirty, the reconciler asks the for [first,last), calls for just that window, keys each row, and feeds it to the existing keyed-LIS diff — so recycling IS CreateNode/FreeNode over the slab free-list (no second pool). Scroll is layout-free (the -ScrollOffset is the content's transform).

is a pluggable (stack / grid / custom — pure, allocation-free). E11-L1: the viewport consumes BOTH seam kinds — an gets the variable-extent estimate-then-correct arrange (measured rows + scroll anchoring) through the SAME property; when the layout is null, the legacy built-in Fenwick extent-table path runs (kept source/behavior-compatible). The user-facing Virtual.List/Grid/Custom/Measured/VariableList/… factories live in FluentGpu.Controls; this record is the engine primitive the reconciler diffs directly (ElementTypeId 6). See design/subsystems/virtualization.md.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Render.AcrylicBackdropMath
files.Summary:
Portable math for the in-app acrylic effect runner (design/subsystems/backdrop-effects-animation.md §2.3 two-pass PushLayer{Acrylic} schedule + §3 IEffectRunner seam, gpu-renderer.md §7 LayerPool). The GPU leaf (Rhi.D3D12 AcrylicCompositor) consumes these values so the region / size-bucket / kernel logic is headless-verifiable in the VerticalSlice while the COM/HLSL stays render-thread-confined in the leaf.

WinUI ground truth: microsoft-ui-xaml AcrylicBrush.h:64 sc_blurRadius = 30.0f, applied as Composition GaussianBlurEffect.BlurAmount (= the gaussian STANDARD DEVIATION, in DIPs) over the backdrop resolved onto the opaque FallbackColor (AcrylicBrush.cpp:500-528). The runner reproduces sigma = BlurSigma·dpiScale physical px with ONE fixed-sigma separable kernel by choosing the snapshot downsample factor instead of the kernel width: down = round(sigmaPhys / KernelSigma), so blurring the 1/down-resolution snapshot with the fixed sigma-7.5 kernel yields the requested full-resolution sigma (30 DIP ⇒ /4 at 100% DPI, /6 at 150%, /8 at 200%).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Render.DrawList
files.Summary: Flat POD command stream consumed by the RHI (SubmitDrawList). The slice grows a single contiguous buffer; the full engine uses double-buffered arenas + clean-span memcpy. SortKeys are recorded in parallel.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Render.FillKind
files.Summary: How a fills its interior.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Render.FocusVisualStyle
files.Summary: The focus-visual brushes (WinUI FocusStrokeColorOuter/Inner + thickness). Passed into the recorder by the host (which reads the theme); default = disabled, so the headless/test paths can opt in.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Render.LayerKind
files.Summary: What a …PopLayer pair composites.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Render.SceneRecorder
files.Summary: Phase 8 (record): walks the retained SceneStore and emits the DrawList. Composites like a browser — each node's geometry is emitted in LOCAL space with a world transform (parent ∘ translate ∘ LocalTransform, scale/rotate about the node's center) and a cumulative opacity, so transform/opacity animate without relayout or re-record of content. Hover/press cross-fade via the eased row; a focused node gets a dual-stroke focus ring.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Render.TextEditStyle
files.Summary: The text-edit decoration brushes (WinUI TextControlSelectionHighlightColor = AccentFillColorSelectedTextBackgroundBrush, TextOnAccentFillColorSelectedTextBrush, and the caret = the text foreground). Passed into the recorder by the host (which reads the theme), like ; default = disabled, so existing paths are untouched.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Rhi.D3D12.D3D12Device
files.Summary: The reference Windows RHI backend (design/subsystems/pal-rhi.md, gpu-renderer.md). Real D3D12: hardware device, DIRECT command queue + fence, a DXGI flip-model swapchain on the HWND, an RTV heap, and per-frame record→submit→present. Step 1 clears; the SDF rounded-rect pipeline and the DirectWrite glyph atlas layer on top.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Rhi.FrameInfo
files.Summary: Per-frame context handed to the device at submit. POD.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Rhi.IGpuDevice
files.Summary: Graphics-first render hardware interface. Zero COM types cross this seam — generational handles + POD + spans only. is the PRIMARY hot path: the leaf walks the POD opcode stream with concrete devirtualized types. D3D12 is the reference backend; Rhi.Headless is the test backend; Metal slots in later behind this seam.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.BrushAnim
files.Summary: An implicit brush transition (WinUI BrushTransition, 83ms): when a LOGICAL state flip re-renders a node with a different Fill/BorderColor/TextColor and the element opted in (BrushTransitionMs), the reconciler captures the previously-DISPLAYED color here and the recorder cross-fades from it to the new resolved color as T advances (linear-light, like the hover/press cross-fade). Sparse side-table — O(transitioning nodes), 0-alloc steady frames.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.ExtentTable
files.Summary:
Per-item extent table for variable-height virtualization: a Fenwick tree / Binary Indexed Tree giving O(log n) (prefix-sum → position a row) and (binary-lift → which item is at a scroll offset), with estimate-then-correct extents (an unmeasured item uses the estimate; on realize+measure corrects it, O(log n)). It persists across frames (only deltas update) and survives a fling because corrections are O(measured-rows). 100k items × 8B ≈ 800KB — within budget.

(virtualization.md §6.2 / layout.md §8.4 — the table MATH. It lives here in FluentGpu.Scene because both Layout and the Reconciler depend on Scene; double-precision prefix sums keep it exact at 100k×100px.)

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.FakeImageDecoder
files.Summary: Deterministic headless/offline decoder: completes on the NEXT (the +1-frame latency contract), reporting the requested target size and synthesizing a stable per-id BGRA pattern so the GPU upload + sample path is exercisable without real codecs or network. Proves the residency/state logic.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.GridSpec
files.Summary: Grid layout spec for a grid container node (sparse side-table, O(grids)). The reconciler writes it from a GridEl; the layout engine resolves column tracks at the final width and auto-flows the cells row-major.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.GridVirtualLayout
files.Summary: Uniform 2-D grid (album/artist card shelves) — virtualizes by ROW: realizes visible-rows × columns, recycles identically. Columns share the cross size; this is the virtualized UniformGridLayout.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.GroupedListVirtualLayout
files.Summary: Grouped flat list with measured rows (E11-L0 grouping hook): the items are a FLATTENED projection — group headers occupy indices of their own (a header is just a measured item KIND, the app-requirements "Liked Songs" shape). must be sorted ascending. Headers seed at , items at ; both correct to measured extents. is the sticky-header support hook: the header that should pin for a given scroll offset (binary search, O(log groups)) — consumers (ItemsView / a custom list) translate that header's realized node by the pin delta at phase 7 (NodeFlags.StickyPinned). Vertical scroll. STATEFUL — create once and reuse (see MeasuredStackVirtualLayout).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.HorizontalGridVirtualLayout
files.Summary: Uniform 2-D grid scrolling HORIZONTALLY (a shelf of fixed-width columns, cells tall) — virtualizes by COLUMN: realizes visible-columns × rows. The horizontal variant of ; pair with VirtualListEl.Horizontal = true.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.IImageDecoder
files.Summary: The decode seam: the portable cache asks a leaf to decode a source to a target size, off the UI thread. The Windows leaf is WIC→GPU (needs-pixels); the headless leaf is deterministic. drains completions onto the UI thread (the +1-frame latency contract: a request is never ready the same frame), invoking onPixels with the bucket pixels then onComplete with the state transition.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.IMeasuredVirtualLayout
files.Summary: The variable-extent (measured) virtualization seam — E11-L0. Folds the Fenwick estimate-then-correct path behind the SAME pluggable seam as the fixed-geometry layouts, so CUSTOM layouts can be variable/sliver-like: unmeasured items report their estimate; at arrange time the layout engine measures each realized row and feeds the real extent back through (O(log n) correction), then re-pins the scroll anchor via / so corrections above the viewport never jump the visible top (virtualization.md §6.2 — estimate-then-correct + anchoring are seam behavior now, not a special case). Built-ins: (the Fenwick variable list), (group headers = a measured item kind + sticky-header hook).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.ISceneBackend
files.Summary: The reconciler's only window onto the retained tree — handle-in / handle-out, POD-only. (The slice implements this directly on .)

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.IVirtualLayout
files.Summary: A pluggable virtualizing layout: PURE, ALLOCATION-FREE arithmetic mapping (itemCount, viewport, scrollOffset) to the realize window + per-item content-space rects. The engine calls it ONLY on realize/arrange frames (a steady in-window scroll is transform-only and never touches it), and the methods return structs / via out-params — so a custom layout costs zero per-frame managed allocation, honoring the engine's core contract. Implement this to make any virtualized layout (lists, card grids, staggered walls, …). Built-ins: , , , , . Variable-extent (measured) layouts implement the widened seam — same contract plus estimate-then-correct feedback.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.ImageBinding
files.Summary: What UseImage returns (media-pipeline.md §5): the stable handle + current load state, so a component can render a spinner / a broken-art fallback / read the failure kind, all without prop-drilling.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.ImageCache
files.Summary: Portable image residency: source→handle dedup, a Pending/Ready/Failed state machine, liveness ref-counting (a pinned/on-screen image is never evicted — the single biggest real-world cache bug, per the research), and an LRU byte budget over decoded images. Decode happens off-thread behind ; applies completions (+1-frame latency). The GPU texture upload is a needs-pixels leaf concern; this owns the logic.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.ImageCompleteHandler
files.Summary: Decode-completion callback: id, success, decoded dims, the failure kind (if any), and the fetch attempt count.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.ImageFailureKind
files.Summary: Why an image decode ended (for diagnostics + app fallbacks). Transient kinds are retried by the decoder before a request is reported ; permanent kinds fail immediately.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.ImageHandle
files.Summary: A generational-free image handle into the (0 = none).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.ImagePriority
files.Summary: Decode urgency (media-pipeline.md §3). Workers drain higher priority first; under backpressure the lowest off-screen lane (Prefetch, then Overscan) is dropped — never .

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.ImageReadyHandler
files.Summary: Forwards decoded PREMULTIPLIED BGRA8 pixels to the GPU backend. The span is valid only for the duration of the synchronous call (it is never stored — the backend copies it into its upload heap), so the cache need not own pixel memory: it flows decoder → cache.Pump → host sink → IGpuDevice.UploadImage in one stack.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.ImageStatusHandler
files.Summary: Image lifecycle notification for app observability (e.g. a retry toast, a broken-art fallback, telemetry). Raised on the UI thread during . attempts is the fetch attempt count (≥1; >1 means transient retries happened).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.InteractionAnim
files.Summary: Eased interaction progress for a node (sparse side-table, O(interacted nodes)). The InteractionAnimator eases HoverT/PressT toward their targets on pointer enter/leave/press; the recorder lerps Fill/Border with them for the WinUI ~83ms brush transition (instead of the instant flag switch).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.InteractionInfo
files.Summary: Hit-test / input column.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.LayoutInput
files.Summary: Layout-input column (flexbox: direction + gap + padding + margin + flex grow/shrink/basis + justify/align + min/max + explicit size + text style).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.LinedFlowLayout
files.Summary: WinUI LinedFlowLayout — the ItemsView photo-wall: items flow left-to-right into uniform-height LINES, each item's width = its aspect ratio × ; a line wraps when the next item would overflow the cross size. Uniform line stride keeps windowing O(1); the per-item line table is (re)built lazily ONLY when (itemCount, crossSize) change — realize/arrange calls stay allocation-free per the seam contract. WinUI defaults: LineSpacing 0, MinItemSpacing 0 (LinedFlowLayout.h:25,27 s_defaultLineSpacing/s_defaultMinItemSpacing); LineHeight has no auto here (WinUI s_defaultLineHeight = NaN = auto-from-first-item; the engine takes it explicitly). Items wider than the cross size clamp to it (single-item line). STATEFUL — create once and reuse.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.MeasuredStackVirtualLayout
files.Summary: The Fenwick variable-extent list behind the seam (E11-L0): every row starts at , gets corrected to its measured extent on realize, and the layout engine re-pins the scroll anchor across corrections. Functionally the engine's legacy Layout = null variable path (kept for compatibility), but USER-REACHABLE: compose it, subclass the idea, or build your own measured layout on the same contract. STATEFUL (owns an ) — create ONCE and reuse across renders (hoist in a UseMemo); the table self-rebuilds only on item-count change.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.NodePaint
files.Summary: Paint column — one cache line of per-node visual state read by the record phase.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.SceneStore
files.Summary: Struct-of-arrays retained RenderNode tree. One spine (gen + free-list) indexes all parallel columns.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.ScrollState
files.Summary: Scroll + virtualization state for a viewport node (marked NodeFlags.Scrollable). There are O(viewports) of these — not one per node — so the store keeps them in a sparse side-table keyed by node index, not a parallel column. Ownership (layout.md §6 / architecture-spec §5.5): Input owns Offset* (clamped to the published content); Layout publishes Content*/Viewport*; the virtualizer owns the Item* / realized-range / anchor fields. Scroll is layout-free: the -ScrollOffset translation is the 's LocalTransform, never a relayout.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.SpanningGridVirtualLayout
files.Summary: Uniform-row grid with ITEM SPANNING (E11-L0): each item occupies SpanOf(i) columns (clamped 1..Columns) and items pack row-major — a span that doesn't fit the current row wraps to the next (the "hero as the first full-width row" shape: SpanOf(0) == Columns). Placement depends only on the item count (not cross size), so the table rebuilds only on count change; windowing binary-searches the monotonic row column. STATEFUL — create once and reuse.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.StackVirtualLayout
files.Summary: Uniform 1-D stack (the WaveeMusic track-list shape) — O(1) windowing. horizontal scrolls X.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.TextEditState
files.Summary: Sparse text-edit state for an editor's TEXT node (side-table, O(editors)): caret geometry + caret-follow scroll + in-flight IME composition span + focus/blink flags. Written by the editing control (UI thread, edit/drag time) and the CaretBlinker phase-7 ticker; the recorder only READS it (plus the pooled decoration rects on ) to emit selection highlight / selected-text recolor / IME underlines / the caret bar — retained scene state, never composed elements (0 alloc in phases 6–13).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.TextMeasureCache
files.Summary: Per-text-node measure cache (layout.md §2.3): a pure-function cache of (text, style, availWidth) → size, so a scoped relayout skips re-shaping a text leaf whose inputs are unchanged. Self-invalidating — any input change makes the stored key not match. Helps the real DirectWrite shaping path; neutral for the headless fake font. Besides the size, the cache retains the face's DECORATION metrics from the same TextMetrics (top-down DIP, the line frame of Baseline — see FluentGpu.Text.TextMetrics): the recorder reads them at record time to place underline/strikethrough bars (NodePaint.TextDecorations) without re-touching the font seam. Filled by the layout engine's measure-miss path; 0 ⇒ the backend reported no face metrics (the recorder falls back to a size-derived approximation).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Scene.VirtualWindowing
files.Summary: Decides whether a scroll offset needs a virtual-window refresh, using the realized overscan as a guard band.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Signals.Computation
files.Summary: A reactive computation — the base for effects, memos and component render-effects. Holds the set of sources it read last run (so it can unlink before re-tracking) and an owner tree of nested computations + cleanups (disposed on re-run / dispose). Subclasses define what running means.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Signals.Effect
files.Summary: A reactive side-effect: runs body once now, then re-runs whenever a signal it READ changes (deferred to the next ). A property binding (signal → scene node) is an Effect; a component render is a specialized effect. Dispose to stop it (also runs registered cleanups).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Signals.FloatSignal
files.Summary: A specialized scalar signal for the hot high-frequency path (slider scrub, scroll offset, progress) — avoids the generic boxing/comparer indirection so a per-pointer-move write is allocation-free. Bind it to a node channel (transform/paint) for a value→pixels update that skips render/reconcile/layout entirely.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Signals.IReadSignal<T>
files.Summary: Read-only view of a reactive value (a or a ).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Signals.ManagedEffect
files.Summary: A subclassable reactive computation for callers that own the run body (e.g. a component render-effect that re-runs Render() + reconciles its subtree). The host calls Schedule behaviour via the runtime; subclasses implement .

files.Title: FluentGpu.Signals.Memo<T>
files.Summary: A derived reactive value (the Solid createMemo / Vue computed): recomputes lazily from the signals it reads, caches the result, and only notifies its own subscribers when the cached value actually changes. It is both a computation (it subscribes upstream) and a source (downstream subscribes to it).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Signals.Prop
files.Summary: A unified reactive channel property: ONE field that is EITHER a static value (re-asserted by the reconciler on every reconcile of the element, iff not bound) OR a reactive bind — a thunk or a concrete signal the reconciler wires into a mount-time effect that writes exactly one scene column and marks its dirty axis. Constructed implicitly: Opacity = 0.5f (static), Opacity = () => f(sig.Value) (derived thunk), Opacity = sig (signal-direct — no user closure). default(Prop<T>) is the static default(T) — never use it to mean "unset"; element property initializers (= 1f, = NaN) run the T conversion and survive with clones (a bound clone stays bound). Kind discrimination is a runtime type test on the single payload ref — no tag byte; the static reconcile path reads only IsBound (one null test). Conversions from the INTERFACE are illegal in C# (CS0552) — through an interface-typed variable use the thunk form chan = () => s.Value.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Signals.Reactive
files.Summary:
The reactive core: a Solid/Preact-style fine-grained reactivity graph. Signals are observable cells; computations (effects, memos, component render-effects) auto-subscribe to the signals they READ during a run, and re-run when a read signal changes. This is the single update mechanism the whole engine is built on — a property binding is an effect at node granularity; a component re-render is an effect at subtree granularity.

Threading: UI-thread-confined (the engine's single render/reconcile thread), so tracking state is . Scheduling is DEFERRED: a signal write marks dependent effects stale and asks the host for a frame; the host drains them once per frame via (phase 3), which keeps reconcile/layout on their phases and the per-frame paint allocation-free.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Signals.ReactiveRuntime
files.Summary: The scheduler: owns the pending-effect queue, the batch depth, and the "a frame is needed" callback the host wires to wake its loop. One per AppHost (so headless tests don't cross-contaminate). Single-threaded.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Signals.ReactiveScope
files.Summary: A stable owner that never re-runs — a container for child computations + cleanups. Used as a component's lifetime scope (its render-effect + bindings live under it) and as the app root. Disposing it cascades to everything it owns.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Signals.Signal<T>
files.Summary: A reactive cell: reading inside a computation subscribes it; writing notifies subscribers (deferred — they re-run on the next ). The unit of state in the engine; a UseState is a Signal whose subscriber is the owning component's render-effect.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.BreakOpp
files.Summary: A UAX #14 line-break opportunity for one UTF-16 position: the break condition before/after the unit (0 = Neutral, 1 = CanBreak, 2 = MayNotBreak, 3 = MustBreak) and whether it is whitespace.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite.DWriteItemizer
files.Summary: DirectWrite itemizer (text.md §4.4): segments text into BiDi × script runs and computes UAX #14 line-break opportunities via IDWriteTextAnalyzer. DirectWrite calls back into the hand-authored callee CCWs (supplies the text) and (collects the runs). COM bound via TerraFX raw pointers; the CCW vtables mirror the ComputeSharp PixelShaderEffect pattern. Single-thread-confined: calls AnalyzeBidi/Script/LineBreakpoints serially on the caller thread.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite.DWriteTextShaper
files.Summary: DirectWrite glyph shaper (text.md §4.4): turns one itemized run (single face + script + direction) into positioned glyphs via IDWriteTextAnalyzer.GetGlyphs + GetGlyphPlacements — real cluster/ligature/kerning/RTL shaping, not per-char design advances. Advances come out in DIP (GetGlyphPlacements is given the DIP em size). Scratch buffers are grown and reused; returns a span into the reused output buffer (valid until the next call). COM via TerraFX raw pointers.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite.DirectWriteBackend
files.Summary: SCAFFOLD (the reference Windows text backend — see design/subsystems/text.md). Will implement the text seam over DirectWrite: itemize (BiDi/script/line-break) → font-fallback → shape, glyph rasterization into the GPU atlas, and the callee-side IDWriteTextAnalysisSource/Sink CCWs. Headless font stub backs the slice today.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite.DirectWriteFontSystem
files.Summary: The DirectWrite text backend () — a thin wrapper over . Every member (measurement AND the editor queries) runs the full itemize → shape → wrap/trim → position pipeline (kerning, ligatures, complex script, UAX #14 line breaking, CJK/emoji font fallback), so the measured box, the hit-tested caret and the selection rects all come from the SAME layout the renderer — which uses the same engine — actually draws. measure ≡ hit-test ≡ render: this parity is what keeps shaped glyphs from overlapping (the divergence that read as bold when the renderer was shaped but measurement was per-char). Touched on the UI thread during phase-6 layout and at edit/drag time; the render thread owns a separate engine instance, so queries here can never corrupt render-side buffers. Query results live in the engine's reused grow-only tables — 0 steady-state allocation per keystroke/pointer-move.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite.LaidCluster
files.Summary: Source mapping for one laid glyph — the hit-test companion to (parallel to minus the synthetic trim ellipsis): the UTF-16 index of the cluster's first source char (taken from the shaper's cluster map, never re-derived from char counts), the glyph's leading pen X and advance on its line, and its line index. Entries are stored in the VISUAL order glyphs were placed (after BiDi L2 reordering), so an in-order scan walks the line left→right.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite.LaidGlyph
files.Summary: One laid-out glyph: post-shaping glyph id, the DWrite face it belongs to (as nint), and its pen position in DIP — X within the paragraph (line-relative origin folded in), Y = its line baseline from the top. is the DIP em size the glyph was shaped at (spans may override the base size) and the index of the it belongs to (−1 = the base style) — the renderer rasterizes at and tints per span (rtb-01 inline runs).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite.LaidLine
files.Summary: One laid-out line: the UTF-16 source range it covers — [StartChar, EndChar), where EndChar is the next line's StartChar (the text length on the last line) — its vertical band (Top/Height), its rendered advance width (including a trim ellipsis), and its window [FirstGlyph, FirstGlyph + GlyphCount) into . Chars dropped by trimming have no cluster entry; queries clamp them to the trimmed edge.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.DirectWrite.TextLayoutEngine
files.Summary: The DirectWrite layout engine (text.md §8): itemize → shape (per run) → wrap (UAX #14 break opportunities) → position, with BiDi L2 reordering per line. Drives BOTH measurement (metrics) and rendering (positioned glyphs) so they layout identically — and the editor queries (//) read the retained per-glyph cluster + per-line tables of the SAME layout, so hit-testing matches rendering exactly. Owns its itemizer + shaper + face cache; reusable, single-thread-confined. Results live in reused grow-only buffers (valid until the next ; queries fire per keystroke/pointer-move with 0 steady-state allocation). v1: design-advance shaping with kerning/ligatures/complex-script; font fallback + color glyphs are layered on in later phases; query edge mapping is leading=left (LTR — BiDi-correct edges arrive with the RTL workstream, but the scan is already cluster-correct and walks the visual order).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.EditDocument
files.Summary:
The text-edit document: a gap buffer of UTF-16 code units (grow by doubling, minimum capacity 64). Every public index is a UTF-16 code-unit offset — the unit WinUI exposes through SelectionStart/MaxLength — so the control layer never converts. Grapheme safety is layered ON TOP via the boundary scanners (//, BCL UAX#29 segmentation — covers surrogate pairs, combining marks, ZWJ emoji, regional indicators): callers that move a caret must land on those boundaries, while the raw / primitives deliberately accept any code-unit range so undo replay can restore exact historic states.

Hard line breaks are a single '\r' (WinUI normalizes CRLF/LF on the way in — see ); '\n' must never be stored. The boundary scanners exploit that invariant: a position right after '\r' is always a grapheme boundary (UAX#29 GB4: break after a control), so they anchor their forward walk at the surrounding line start instead of offset 0 — O(line length), and text fields are short.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.GlyphPlacement
files.Summary: One shaped glyph in logical→visual order: the POST-shaping glyph id (NOT a codepoint), its DIP advance, the pen offset (DIP), and the first source text index it maps to (its cluster start, for wrap/hit-test/selection). Produced by the shaper (text.md §4.4); consumed by the layout engine (wrap/measure) and the glyph rasterizer.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.IAdvanceSource
files.Summary: A per-glyph advance source for the shared wrap/measure math. Implemented as a struct so the generic wrap routines below monomorphize and inline the advance lookup (zero allocation, no virtual dispatch). The advance is the DIP advance for one UTF-16 code unit (design advance × size/em). The render side (D3D12 GlyphRenderer) and the measure side (DirectWriteFontSystem) each supply their own struct over the SAME DirectWrite design advances, so measurement and rendering break lines identically.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.IFontSystem
files.Summary: Text seam. DirectWrite implements it on Windows; Text.Headless provides a deterministic stub for tests. The shaper resolves BiDi/script/fallback and returns FINAL device-space glyph positions in visual order; the renderer treats glyph runs as opaque positioned quads. Beyond measurement, the seam answers the editor queries — hit-test, caret geometry, selection rects — from the SAME layout pipeline (identical wrapping, identical advances), so hit-testing matches rendering exactly. For all members maxWidth is the wrap width ( = no wrap), interpreted exactly as interprets it.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.ITextItemizer
files.Summary: Text itemization seam (text.md §4.4): segment a string into BiDi × script runs and compute per-position line-break opportunities (UAX #14). The DirectWrite leaf implements it over IDWriteTextAnalyzer + the callee IDWriteTextAnalysisSource/Sink CCWs; a portable Unicode itemizer is the cross-platform follow-up. The shaper (and, later, the layout engine's wrap) consume this output so measure and render segment identically.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.ItemRun
files.Summary: One itemized run: a maximal span of the text with a single BiDi level AND a single script — the unit the shaper consumes (one run = one face/script/direction). Produced by intersecting the BiDi-level runs with the script runs. / are the opaque DirectWrite script id + shaping flags, carried as portable scalars so this seam stays Windows/COM-free.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.LineBreaker
files.Summary: THE single source of truth for line breaking, ellipsis fitting, and wrapped-box measurement — extracted verbatim from the D3D12 GlyphRenderer shaping so the layout MEASURE path and the glyph RENDER path can never disagree on where lines break or how tall a wrapped block is (the GDI-measure/DWrite-render mismatch that overlapped wrapped content). Pure, COM-free, allocation-free over a + a struct . wrap uses the TextWrap enum value (0 = NoWrap, 1 = Wrap, 2 = WrapWholeWords); trim uses the TextTrim enum value (0 = None). LTR design-advance positioning (matches the v1 renderer).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.TextEditCommand
files.Summary: Editor commands in DOCUMENT terms. handles every document-order command; the vertical/visual ones (Up/Down/Page*) and the seam-touching ones (Copy/Cut/Paste need the clipboard PAL, Commit/Cancel are control semantics) intentionally fall through to the control layer.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.TextEditCore
files.Summary:
The portable text-editing state machine: an + caret/selection + bounded undo, with WinUI TextBox semantics made exact. Pure C# over the BCL — no PAL/Scene/Render types — so the same core drives TextBox/PasswordBox/NumberBox/AutoSuggestBox/ComboBox on Windows AND headless checks.

Contracts every mutation upholds: caret positions are grapheme boundaries (never inside a surrogate pair, ZWJ emoji, or combining sequence); / stay in [0, Length]; every document change bumps Doc.Version; undo/redo restore the EXACT before/after caret+anchor pairs. Caret movement and selection ops allocate nothing — strings materialize only at the user-gesture edge (copy, undo capture, paste normalization).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.TextEditKeymap
files.Summary: The WinUI TextBox key map as one pure function — no state, so it is trivially testable and shared by every editable control. (None, false) means "not ours": the key must BUBBLE, which is load-bearing for Up/Down/PageUp/PageDown in single-line fields (AutoSuggestBox list navigation rides the unhandled key).

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.TextKeyBinding
files.Summary: A resolved key chord: the command plus whether Shift extends the selection while it executes.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.TextMetrics
files.Summary: Result of measuring a run: the box the shaped glyphs occupy + the baseline offset from the top, plus the face's decoration metrics — all vertical values measured DOWN from the line top, the same frame as . / place the underline bar and the strikethrough bar (it reuses the underline thickness). These feed text decorations; backends without face metrics (the experimental GDI path) leave them 0.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.TextSeam
files.Summary: Process-default for engine layers that have no host seam to receive an instance through — concretely the input dispatcher's read-only text-selection/hyperlink gestures (rtb-02), which need the editor queries but are constructed by hosts (AppHost) that predate them. Set by each backend's constructor; last-constructed wins (the established InputHooks.Current channel-default convention). Consumers prefer an explicitly-wired instance and fall back here.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.TextStyle
files.Summary:
The resolved style a text run is measured/shaped under. is the NUMERIC font weight (WinUI FontWeight — the int IS the DWRITE_FONT_WEIGHT, 1..999; callers resolve sugar like Bold→700 before constructing; 0 is treated as 400). is WinUI TextElement.CharacterSpacing — tracking in 1/1000 em, applied per glyph after shaping. (DIP; NaN/≤0 = font-natural) resolves per (WinUI LineStackingStrategy), and (WinUI TextLineBounds) selects the reported line box (Full = ascent..descent, Tight = cap-height..baseline). Record equality over ALL fields is load-bearing: TextStyle is the TextMeasureCache key, so every layout-affecting input must live here.

<p><xref href="FluentGpu.Text.TextStyle.SpanRunId" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref> (0 = plain single-style run) keys a <xref href="FluentGpu.Foundation.SpanRunTable.Shared" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref> run whose
per-range <xref href="FluentGpu.Foundation.SpanStyle" data-throw-if-not-resolved="false"></xref> overlays (weight/size/family/color/decorations) apply over this base style — the
rtb-01 inline-run model: the WHOLE text still shapes as ONE flow (one wrap pass), exactly like a WinUI paragraph's
inline collection. Carried here (not as a separate seam argument) so every existing layout/measure/query path —
and every cache keyed on TextStyle — is span-aware without signature churn; a span-content change mints a fresh
id, so the measure cache and the shaped-run cache self-invalidate.</p>

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.TextUndoOp
files.Summary: One reversible edit against an : the op replaced with at . Undo = remove Inserted.Length at Start, re-insert Removed, restore /; redo is the inverse with the After pair — so undo/redo restore the EXACT caret/selection the user saw, not a recomputed approximation.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Text.TextUndoStack
files.Summary: Bounded undo/redo for the text editor (cap 512 ops, oldest dropped — a hard memory ceiling per field, like RichEdit). Coalescing makes one undo step out of a typing burst: a new op merges into the open top op when the kinds match AND the positions are contiguous (typing extends forward from the previous insert's end; backspace walks backward, forward-delete stays in place) AND no selection was replaced AND the caller did not seal. Paste, IME commit, selection-replacing edits, and any caret relocation seal the run ( or sealRun: true on ) so they become their own undo steps — the WinUI/RichEdit feel: "abc" undoes in one step, a paste in its own step.

files.Title: FluentGpu.Win32.Interop.Win32InteropMarker
files.Summary: SCAFFOLD. Owner of the hand-vtable COM layer (see design/subsystems/com-interop.md): vendored ComputeSharp ComPtr<T> + ClangSharp-generated D3D12/DXGI/D2D1 bindings, and the generated DComp/DWrite/UIA vtables (consume = lpVtbl[n] calli; implement = static [UnmanagedCallersOnly(CallConvMemberFunction)] + GCHandle). Empty until the Windows leaves are implemented; the headless slice does not need it.
